description: All transactions posted to a specific GL account in a date range
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.GlCode, m.Description AS AccountName, t.JnlDate, t.Journal, t.Source, t.Reference, t.Comment, t.EntryValue, t.CurrencyValue, t.PostCurrency, t.Origin, t.SubModRef, t.SubModSupplier, t.SubModCustomer, t.SubModApInvoice, t.SubModArInvoice FROM GenTransaction t LEFT JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = t.Company AND m.GlCode = t.GlCode WHERE t.GlCode = %(gl_code)s AND t.JnlDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY t.JnlDate DESC, t.Journal DESC
embedding_text: Account-level activity from GenTransaction for a specific GlCode within a date range, joined to GenMaster for the account name. Shows source, reference, sub-module link fields. Used for account analysis and drill-down.
example_questions: GL account activity for account 400000
Show all postings to account X
Ledger for one account between two dates

description: GL journal summary by posting currency
sql: SELECT t.PostCurrency, COUNT(*) AS EntryCount, SUM(CASE WHEN t.CurrencyValue >= 0 THEN t.CurrencyValue ELSE 0 END) AS TotalDebitsForeign, SUM(CASE WHEN t.CurrencyValue < 0 THEN -t.CurrencyValue ELSE 0 END) AS TotalCreditsForeign, SUM(CASE WHEN t.EntryValue >= 0 THEN t.EntryValue ELSE 0 END) AS TotalDebitsLocal, SUM(CASE WHEN t.EntryValue < 0 THEN -t.EntryValue ELSE 0 END) AS TotalCreditsLocal FROM GenTransaction t WHERE t.JnlDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND t.PostCurrency IS NOT NULL AND t.PostCurrency <> '' GROUP BY t.PostCurrency ORDER BY EntryCount DESC
embedding_text: Summarises GenTransaction by PostCurrency showing both foreign currency value (CurrencyValue) and local value (EntryValue) totals. Used for multi-currency GL exposure reporting.
example_questions: Journals in foreign currency
GL postings by currency
Multi-currency entries summary

description: Journal entry count and total value grouped by source module
sql: SELECT t.Source, COUNT(*) AS EntryCount, SUM(CASE WHEN t.EntryValue >= 0 THEN t.EntryValue ELSE 0 END) AS TotalDebits, SUM(CASE WHEN t.EntryValue < 0 THEN -t.EntryValue ELSE 0 END) AS TotalCredits, SUM(t.EntryValue) AS NetValue FROM GenTransaction t WHERE t.JnlDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s GROUP BY t.Source ORDER BY EntryCount DESC
embedding_text: Aggregates GenTransaction entries by Source code (G=General, A=AR, P=AP, C=Cash, I=Inventory etc.) to show which modules feed the GL. Counts entries and totals debits, credits and net value.
example_questions: Journals by source module AR AP Cash
How many entries per source code?
GL postings breakdown by feeder module

description: List of recurring journal templates from GenRecJnlCtl with their detail lines
sql: SELECT c.JournalCode, c.Reference, c.Notation, c.NumOfEntries, c.DebitAmount, c.CreditAmount, c.PostFrequency, c.NextPostYear, c.NextPostPeriod, c.LastYearPosted, c.LastPeriodPosted, c.Authorised, c.AuthorisedBy FROM GenRecJnlCtl c ORDER BY c.JournalCode
embedding_text: Returns recurring journal templates set up in GenRecJnlCtl. Shows posting frequency, last posted period and next scheduled period. Useful for month-end close planning and accrual review.
example_questions: Recurring journal entries list
Show recurring journals
Recurring accruals setup

description: All transactions posted to a suspense GL account that need clearing
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.GlCode, m.Description, t.JnlDate, t.Journal, t.Source, t.Reference, t.Comment, t.EntryValue, t.PostCurrency, t.SubModRef FROM GenTransaction t INNER JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = t.Company AND m.GlCode = t.GlCode WHERE t.GlCode = %(suspense_account)s AND t.JnlDate <= GETDATE() ORDER BY t.JnlDate DESC
embedding_text: Returns all postings to the suspense account from GenTransaction so they can be investigated and cleared before period close. Pass the suspense GL code as parameter.
example_questions: Suspense account transactions
Show all entries posted to suspense account
Items in clearing account

description: Highest value journal entries in a date range
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.GlCode, m.Description, t.JnlDate, t.Journal, t.Source, t.Reference, t.Comment, t.EntryValue, t.PostCurrency, t.Origin FROM GenTransaction t LEFT JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = t.Company AND m.GlCode = t.GlCode WHERE t.JnlDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY ABS(t.EntryValue) DESC
embedding_text: Returns the top N journal entries by absolute value from GenTransaction in a date range. Useful for materiality analysis, audit sampling, and unusual transaction detection.
example_questions: Top 10 highest value journal entries
Biggest journals last month
Largest GL postings this quarter

description: Budget vs actual by cost centre (analysis section)
sql: SELECT s.SectionNumber, s.SectionCode, s.Description AS CostCentreDesc, s.AccountType, b.BudgetType, b.Budget1+b.Budget2+b.Budget3+b.Budget4+b.Budget5+b.Budget6+b.Budget7+b.Budget8+b.Budget9+b.Budget10+b.Budget11+b.Budget12 AS BudgetYearTotal FROM GenSection s LEFT JOIN GenAnalysisBudget b ON b.SectionNumber = s.SectionNumber AND b.SectionCode = s.SectionCode AND b.AccountType = s.AccountType WHERE (%(section_code)s IS NULL OR s.SectionCode = %(section_code)s) ORDER BY s.SectionNumber, s.SectionCode
embedding_text: Cost-centre (section) level budget from GenAnalysisBudget joined to GenSection for descriptions. Aggregates 12 periods into yearly budget total. Optionally filter by section code.
example_questions: Budget variance by cost centre
Cost centre budgets
Section wise budgets

description: Monthly budget consumption percentage (actual / budget) per account
sql: SELECT m.GlCode, m.Description, m.AccountType, %(period)s AS Period, CASE %(period)s WHEN 1 THEN b.Budget1 WHEN 2 THEN b.Budget2 WHEN 3 THEN b.Budget3 WHEN 4 THEN b.Budget4 WHEN 5 THEN b.Budget5 WHEN 6 THEN b.Budget6 WHEN 7 THEN b.Budget7 WHEN 8 THEN b.Budget8 WHEN 9 THEN b.Budget9 WHEN 10 THEN b.Budget10 WHEN 11 THEN b.Budget11 WHEN 12 THEN b.Budget12 END AS BudgetForPeriod, ISNULL(CASE %(period)s WHEN 1 THEN h.ClosingBalPer1 - h.BeginYearBalance WHEN 2 THEN h.ClosingBalPer2 - h.ClosingBalPer1 WHEN 3 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 - h.ClosingBalPer2 WHEN 4 THEN h.ClosingBalPer4 - h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 5 THEN h.ClosingBalPer5 - h.ClosingBalPer4 WHEN 6 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 - h.ClosingBalPer5 WHEN 7 THEN h.ClosingBalPer7 - h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 8 THEN h.ClosingBalPer8 - h.ClosingBalPer7 WHEN 9 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 - h.ClosingBalPer8 WHEN 10 THEN h.ClosingBalPer10 - h.ClosingBalPer9 WHEN 11 THEN h.ClosingBalPer11 - h.ClosingBalPer10 WHEN 12 THEN h.ClosingBalPer12 - h.ClosingBalPer11 END, 0) AS ActualForPeriod, CASE WHEN (CASE %(period)s WHEN 1 THEN b.Budget1 WHEN 2 THEN b.Budget2 WHEN 3 THEN b.Budget3 WHEN 4 THEN b.Budget4 WHEN 5 THEN b.Budget5 WHEN 6 THEN b.Budget6 WHEN 7 THEN b.Budget7 WHEN 8 THEN b.Budget8 WHEN 9 THEN b.Budget9 WHEN 10 THEN b.Budget10 WHEN 11 THEN b.Budget11 WHEN 12 THEN b.Budget12 END) <> 0 THEN 100.0 * ISNULL(CASE %(period)s WHEN 1 THEN h.ClosingBalPer1 - h.BeginYearBalance WHEN 2 THEN h.ClosingBalPer2 - h.ClosingBalPer1 WHEN 3 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 - h.ClosingBalPer2 WHEN 4 THEN h.ClosingBalPer4 - h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 5 THEN h.ClosingBalPer5 - h.ClosingBalPer4 WHEN 6 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 - h.ClosingBalPer5 WHEN 7 THEN h.ClosingBalPer7 - h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 8 THEN h.ClosingBalPer8 - h.ClosingBalPer7 WHEN 9 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 - h.ClosingBalPer8 WHEN 10 THEN h.ClosingBalPer10 - h.ClosingBalPer9 WHEN 11 THEN h.ClosingBalPer11 - h.ClosingBalPer10 WHEN 12 THEN h.ClosingBalPer12 - h.ClosingBalPer11 END, 0) / (CASE %(period)s WHEN 1 THEN b.Budget1 WHEN 2 THEN b.Budget2 WHEN 3 THEN b.Budget3 WHEN 4 THEN b.Budget4 WHEN 5 THEN b.Budget5 WHEN 6 THEN b.Budget6 WHEN 7 THEN b.Budget7 WHEN 8 THEN b.Budget8 WHEN 9 THEN b.Budget9 WHEN 10 THEN b.Budget10 WHEN 11 THEN b.Budget11 WHEN 12 THEN b.Budget12 END) ELSE NULL END AS ConsumptionPct FROM GenMaster m INNER JOIN GenBudgets b ON b.Company = m.Company AND b.GlCode = m.GlCode AND b.BudgetType = %(budget_type)s AND b.BudgetNumber = %(budget_number)s LEFT JOIN GenHistory h ON h.Company = m.Company AND h.GlCode = m.GlCode AND h.GlYear = %(gl_year)s ORDER BY m.GlCode
embedding_text: Per-account, per-period budget consumption percentage: pulls budget for the period from GenBudgets and actual period movement from GenHistory, then computes (actual / budget) * 100. Joined to GenMaster for descriptions.
example_questions: Monthly budget consumption percentage
Burn rate against budget
Budget utilization for current month

description: P&L budget vs actual variance for the year by GL account
sql: SELECT m.GlCode, m.Description, m.AccountType, m.GlGroup, b.Budget1, b.Budget2, b.Budget3, b.Budget4, b.Budget5, b.Budget6, b.Budget7, b.Budget8, b.Budget9, b.Budget10, b.Budget11, b.Budget12, b.Budget1+b.Budget2+b.Budget3+b.Budget4+b.Budget5+b.Budget6+b.Budget7+b.Budget8+b.Budget9+b.Budget10+b.Budget11+b.Budget12 AS BudgetYearTotal, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer12 - h.BeginYearBalance, 0) AS ActualYearMovement, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer12 - h.BeginYearBalance, 0) - (b.Budget1+b.Budget2+b.Budget3+b.Budget4+b.Budget5+b.Budget6+b.Budget7+b.Budget8+b.Budget9+b.Budget10+b.Budget11+b.Budget12) AS Variance FROM GenMaster m LEFT JOIN GenBudgets b ON b.Company = m.Company AND b.GlCode = m.GlCode AND b.BudgetType = %(budget_type)s AND b.BudgetNumber = %(budget_number)s LEFT JOIN GenHistory h ON h.Company = m.Company AND h.GlCode = m.GlCode AND h.GlYear = %(gl_year)s WHERE m.AccountType IN ('R','E') ORDER BY m.AccountType, m.GlCode
embedding_text: P&L budget vs actual: pulls 12-period budget from GenBudgets and actual year movement from GenHistory (ClosingBalPer12 minus BeginYearBalance), computing variance per account. Filtered to revenue and expense accounts.
example_questions: P&L variance – actual vs budget
Budget vs actual report
Show variance for the year

description: Historical exchange rate changes for a currency
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s h.Currency, h.DateRateChange, h.TimeRateChange, h.ConvRate, h.MulDiv, h.Triangulated, h.TriangCurrency, h.Operator, h.ReasonForChange FROM GenHistCur h WHERE h.Currency = %(currency)s AND h.DateRateChange BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY h.DateRateChange DESC, h.TimeRateChange DESC
embedding_text: Historical conversion rates from GenHistCur for one currency across a date range. Shows operator who changed the rate and the reason. Used for FX rate audit and reval verification.
example_questions: Exchange rate history for USD
Show FX rate changes for EUR
Currency rate audit

description: Group P&L and Balance Sheet roll-up at group/category level across companies
sql: SELECT m.AccountType, CASE m.AccountType WHEN 'A' THEN 'Asset' WHEN 'L' THEN 'Liability' WHEN 'C' THEN 'Capital' WHEN 'R' THEN 'Revenue' WHEN 'E' THEN 'Expense' END AS TypeDesc, m.GlGroup, g.Description AS GroupDesc, SUM(ISNULL(CASE %(period)s WHEN 1 THEN h.ClosingBalPer1 WHEN 2 THEN h.ClosingBalPer2 WHEN 3 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 4 THEN h.ClosingBalPer4 WHEN 5 THEN h.ClosingBalPer5 WHEN 6 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 7 THEN h.ClosingBalPer7 WHEN 8 THEN h.ClosingBalPer8 WHEN 9 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 WHEN 10 THEN h.ClosingBalPer10 WHEN 11 THEN h.ClosingBalPer11 WHEN 12 THEN h.ClosingBalPer12 END, 0)) AS GroupTotal FROM GenMaster m LEFT JOIN GenHistory h ON h.Company = m.Company AND h.GlCode = m.GlCode AND h.GlYear = %(gl_year)s LEFT JOIN GenGroups g ON g.Company = m.Company AND g.GlGroup = m.GlGroup GROUP BY m.AccountType, m.GlGroup, g.Description ORDER BY m.AccountType, m.GlGroup
embedding_text: Group-level P&L and BS roll-up aggregated by AccountType and GlGroup across all companies. Joins GenMaster, GenHistory, GenGroups. Used for board-level summary statements.
example_questions: Group P&L and Balance Sheet
Consolidated financials by group
Roll-up by account category

description: Group trial balance combining all companies (uses Company field in GenMaster/GenHistory)
sql: SELECT m.Company, m.GlCode, m.Description, m.AccountType, m.GlGroup, ISNULL(h.BeginYearBalance, 0) AS OpeningBalance, ISNULL(CASE %(period)s WHEN 1 THEN h.ClosingBalPer1 WHEN 2 THEN h.ClosingBalPer2 WHEN 3 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 4 THEN h.ClosingBalPer4 WHEN 5 THEN h.ClosingBalPer5 WHEN 6 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 7 THEN h.ClosingBalPer7 WHEN 8 THEN h.ClosingBalPer8 WHEN 9 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 WHEN 10 THEN h.ClosingBalPer10 WHEN 11 THEN h.ClosingBalPer11 WHEN 12 THEN h.ClosingBalPer12 END, 0) AS ClosingBalance FROM GenMaster m LEFT JOIN GenHistory h ON h.Company = m.Company AND h.GlCode = m.GlCode AND h.GlYear = %(gl_year)s ORDER BY m.Company, m.AccountType, m.GlCode
embedding_text: Group TB across all companies: combines GenMaster and GenHistory keyed by Company + GlCode. Caller can then aggregate by GL group to produce consolidated balances.
example_questions: Group trial balance for all companies
Consolidated TB by company
All entities trial balance

description: Intercompany journal entries that need elimination during consolidation
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.Company AS PrimaryCompany, t.SecondaryCompany, t.GlCode, m.Description, t.JnlDate, t.Journal, t.Source, t.Reference, t.EntryValue, t.PostCurrency, t.InterCompanyTrx, t.GroupInterCompany FROM GenTransaction t LEFT JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = t.Company AND m.GlCode = t.GlCode WHERE t.JnlDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND (t.InterCompanyTrx = 'Y' OR t.GroupInterCompany = 'Y' OR t.SecondaryCompany IS NOT NULL) ORDER BY t.Company, t.SecondaryCompany, t.JnlDate
embedding_text: Returns intercompany transactions in GenTransaction (where InterCompanyTrx or GroupInterCompany flags are set). Provides the data needed to prepare elimination journals at the group consolidation level.
example_questions: Intercompany eliminations entries
Show interco journals for the period
Consolidation elimination data

description: Unrealized forex gain/loss by GL account using last revaluation rate
sql: SELECT r.Currency, r.Company, r.GlCode, m.Description, r.GlYear, r.GlPeriod, r.LastRate, l.AveExchangeRate, l.MthEndExchangeRate, l.RevaluationType, l.RevaluationDate FROM GenLastRevalRate r INNER JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = r.Company AND m.GlCode = r.GlCode LEFT JOIN GenRevalLog l ON l.Company = r.Company AND l.PostCurrency = r.Currency AND l.GlYear = r.GlYear AND l.GlPeriod = r.GlPeriod WHERE r.Currency = %(currency)s AND r.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND r.GlPeriod = %(period)s ORDER BY r.GlCode
embedding_text: Joins GenLastRevalRate (rate used at last revaluation per account) with GenRevalLog (avg and month-end rate applied at that revaluation). Used to compute and review unrealized forex gain/loss positions.
example_questions: Unrealized forex gain/loss by account
Show revaluation rates for USD accounts
FX position by GL account

description: Audit trail for a specific journal — who posted it, when, and what changed
sql: SELECT c.GlYear, c.GlPeriod, c.GlJournal, c.JournalDate, c.Source, c.JournalSource, c.Operator AS PostedByOperator, c.AuthorisedBy, c.PostedBy, c.Authorised, c.JnlStatus, c.PostDate, c.Reference, c.Notation, c.DebitAmount, c.CreditAmount, c.NumOfEntries, r.AllocatedByOper, r.AllocatedDate, r.AllocatedByProgram, r.CalledFromProgram, r.Status, r.ClosedDate FROM GenJournalCtl c LEFT JOIN GenJournalRef r ON r.GlYear = c.GlYear AND r.GlPeriod = c.GlPeriod AND r.GlJournal = c.GlJournal WHERE c.GlJournal = %(journal)s AND c.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND c.GlPeriod = %(period)s
embedding_text: Audit trail for a single journal: combines GenJournalCtl (who posted, authorised, when, status) with GenJournalRef (who allocated the journal number, from which program, when closed). Used for forensic queries.
example_questions: Audit trail: who posted journal 12345?
Who created GL journal X?
Show full audit log for one journal

description: Journals that were amended or marked deleted from the data API amendment journal
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s a.RunDateTime, a.TableName, a.ChangeFlag, a.BeforeValues, a.AfterValues, a.Operator, a.SysproSource, a.ApplicationName FROM AdmDataApiAmendJnl a WHERE a.RunDateTime BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND (a.TableName IN ('GenJournalCtl','GenJournalDetail','GenTransaction') OR a.ChangeFlag = 'D') ORDER BY a.RunDateTime DESC
embedding_text: Looks at AdmDataApiAmendJnl for changes against GL tables (GenJournalCtl, GenJournalDetail, GenTransaction) including deletes (ChangeFlag = 'D'). Useful for audit of amended or removed journals.
example_questions: Deleted or amended journals in last month
Show journal changes audit log
Were any GL entries deleted recently?

description: Drill from a GL account balance to the sub-ledger transaction that created it (AP/AR/Cash/SO/Job)
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.GlCode, m.Description AS GlAccount, t.JnlDate, t.Journal, t.Source, t.Origin, t.Reference, t.EntryValue, t.SubModSupplier, t.SubModCustomer, t.SubModApInvoice, t.SubModArInvoice, t.SubModCheck, t.SubModBank, t.SubModSalesOrder, t.SubModJob, t.SubModAsset, t.SubModRef, t.OriginZoomKey FROM GenTransaction t LEFT JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = t.Company AND m.GlCode = t.GlCode WHERE t.GlCode = %(gl_code)s AND t.JnlDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND (t.SubModSupplier IS NOT NULL OR t.SubModCustomer IS NOT NULL OR t.SubModApInvoice IS NOT NULL OR t.SubModArInvoice IS NOT NULL) ORDER BY t.JnlDate DESC, t.Journal DESC
embedding_text: GL-to-sub-ledger drill: returns GenTransaction lines for a GL code along with all SubMod* link fields (supplier, customer, invoice, cheque, bank, sales order, job, asset). Use SubMod* values to navigate to the originating AP/AR/Cash/SO/Job record.
example_questions: Drill from GL balance to AR sub-ledger
Show origin of postings in GL account 200100
Find sub-ledger entry behind a GL line

description: GL journals posted in foreign (non-base) currency
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s c.GlYear, c.GlPeriod, c.GlJournal, c.JournalDate, c.Source, c.Operator, c.Reference, c.Currency, c.DebitAmount, c.CreditAmount FROM GenJournalCtl c WHERE c.JournalDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND c.Currency IS NOT NULL AND c.Currency <> '' AND c.Currency <> %(base_currency)s ORDER BY c.JournalDate DESC
embedding_text: Journals in GenJournalCtl whose Currency is set and differs from the base currency. Pass base currency (e.g. 'USD') as parameter. Used for multi-currency exposure audit.
example_questions: Journals in foreign currency
Non-base currency GL entries
Show forex GL journals

description: GL journals grouped by approval status
sql: SELECT c.Authorised, c.AuthorisedBy, COUNT(*) AS JournalCount, SUM(c.DebitAmount) AS TotalDebit, SUM(c.CreditAmount) AS TotalCredit FROM GenJournalCtl c WHERE c.JournalDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s GROUP BY c.Authorised, c.AuthorisedBy ORDER BY JournalCount DESC
embedding_text: Aggregates GenJournalCtl by Authorised flag (Y/N) and AuthorisedBy operator. Shows how many journals each user authorised and the totals. Used for approval audit.
example_questions: Journals by approval status
Who approved most journals?
Approval audit on GL journals

description: Posted journals that were never authorised (control violation)
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s c.GlYear, c.GlPeriod, c.GlJournal, c.JournalDate, c.PostDate, c.Source, c.Operator, c.Reference, c.DebitAmount, c.CreditAmount, c.NumOfEntries, c.JnlStatus FROM GenJournalCtl c WHERE c.JournalDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND (c.Authorised <> 'Y' OR c.Authorised IS NULL) AND c.PostDate IS NOT NULL ORDER BY c.JournalDate DESC
embedding_text: Lists GL journals that were posted (PostDate set) without authorisation (Authorised <> 'Y'). Indicates a segregation-of-duties or workflow gap. Pulled from GenJournalCtl.
example_questions: Show journals with missing approval
Posted but unapproved journals
Approval bypass audit

description: Balance sheet showing closing balances for asset, liability, and capital accounts
sql: SELECT m.AccountType, CASE m.AccountType WHEN 'A' THEN 'Asset' WHEN 'L' THEN 'Liability' WHEN 'C' THEN 'Capital' END AS AccountTypeDesc, m.GlGroup, g.Description AS GroupDescription, m.GlCode, m.Description, ISNULL(CASE %(period)s WHEN 1 THEN h.ClosingBalPer1 WHEN 2 THEN h.ClosingBalPer2 WHEN 3 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 4 THEN h.ClosingBalPer4 WHEN 5 THEN h.ClosingBalPer5 WHEN 6 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 7 THEN h.ClosingBalPer7 WHEN 8 THEN h.ClosingBalPer8 WHEN 9 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 WHEN 10 THEN h.ClosingBalPer10 WHEN 11 THEN h.ClosingBalPer11 WHEN 12 THEN h.ClosingBalPer12 END, 0) AS ClosingBalance FROM GenMaster m LEFT JOIN GenHistory h ON h.Company = m.Company AND h.GlCode = m.GlCode AND h.GlYear = %(gl_year)s LEFT JOIN GenGroups g ON g.Company = m.Company AND g.GlGroup = m.GlGroup WHERE m.AccountType IN ('A','L','C') ORDER BY m.AccountType, m.GlGroup, m.GlCode
embedding_text: Balance sheet from GenMaster filtered to asset (A), liability (L), and capital (C) account types, with closing balances pulled from GenHistory for the requested period. Joined to GenGroups for group descriptions. Used for monthly/yearly financial position reporting.
example_questions: Balance sheet as of year end
Show me balance sheet for period 12 2025
Assets liabilities and capital position

description: Cash flow statement using the direct method based on Cash Book transactions
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN t.TrnType IN ('R') THEN 'Operating Inflows' WHEN t.TrnType IN ('P','C') THEN 'Operating Outflows' WHEN t.TrnType = 'A' THEN 'Adjustments' WHEN t.TrnType = 'D' THEN 'Deposits' ELSE 'Other' END AS FlowCategory, t.TrnType, t.Bank, COUNT(*) AS TransactionCount, SUM(t.TrnValue) AS TotalValue, SUM(t.CurrencyValue) AS LocalValue FROM CshTransactions t WHERE t.TrnDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND ISNULL(t.VoidFlag, 'N') <> 'Y' GROUP BY t.TrnType, t.Bank ORDER BY t.TrnType, t.Bank
embedding_text: Direct method cash flow statement aggregating CshTransactions by transaction type (Receipt, Payment, Adjustment) over a date range. Provides bank-wise inflow/outflow breakdown. Excludes voided transactions.
example_questions: Cash flow statement direct method
Show cash inflows and outflows for last quarter
Direct cash flow report

description: Indirect cash flow building blocks: net income plus non-cash adjustments and working capital changes
sql: SELECT m.AccountType, m.ExpenseType, m.GlGroup, m.GlCode, m.Description, ISNULL(CASE %(period)s WHEN 1 THEN h.ClosingBalPer1 - h.BeginYearBalance WHEN 2 THEN h.ClosingBalPer2 - h.ClosingBalPer1 WHEN 3 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 - h.ClosingBalPer2 WHEN 4 THEN h.ClosingBalPer4 - h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 5 THEN h.ClosingBalPer5 - h.ClosingBalPer4 WHEN 6 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 - h.ClosingBalPer5 WHEN 7 THEN h.ClosingBalPer7 - h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 8 THEN h.ClosingBalPer8 - h.ClosingBalPer7 WHEN 9 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 - h.ClosingBalPer8 WHEN 10 THEN h.ClosingBalPer10 - h.ClosingBalPer9 WHEN 11 THEN h.ClosingBalPer11 - h.ClosingBalPer10 WHEN 12 THEN h.ClosingBalPer12 - h.ClosingBalPer11 END, 0) AS PeriodMovement FROM GenMaster m LEFT JOIN GenHistory h ON h.Company = m.Company AND h.GlCode = m.GlCode AND h.GlYear = %(gl_year)s WHERE (m.AccountType IN ('R','E') OR m.GlGroup LIKE %(working_capital_group)s) ORDER BY m.AccountType, m.GlCode
embedding_text: Indirect method cash flow inputs: period movement of P&L accounts (R, E) plus working capital movement (matched by GlGroup pattern). Caller adds back depreciation and adjustments. Reads GenHistory and GenMaster.
example_questions: Cash flow indirect method for last quarter
Indirect cash flow statement components
Working capital movement for period

description: Statement of changes in equity (capital accounts) for the year
sql: SELECT m.GlCode, m.Description, m.GlGroup, ISNULL(h.BeginYearBalance, 0) AS OpeningBalance, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer1, 0) AS EndPer1, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer2, 0) AS EndPer2, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer3, 0) AS EndPer3, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer4, 0) AS EndPer4, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer5, 0) AS EndPer5, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer6, 0) AS EndPer6, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer7, 0) AS EndPer7, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer8, 0) AS EndPer8, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer9, 0) AS EndPer9, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer10, 0) AS EndPer10, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer11, 0) AS EndPer11, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer12, 0) AS EndPer12, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer12, 0) - ISNULL(h.BeginYearBalance, 0) AS NetMovement FROM GenMaster m LEFT JOIN GenHistory h ON h.Company = m.Company AND h.GlCode = m.GlCode AND h.GlYear = %(gl_year)s WHERE m.AccountType = 'C' ORDER BY m.GlCode
embedding_text: Statement of changes in equity for all Capital (AccountType = 'C') GL accounts. Shows opening, period-end balances for 12 periods, and net movement over the year. Useful for shareholder reporting and year-end disclosures.
example_questions: Statement of changes in equity
How did our equity change in 2025?
Capital account movement for the year

description: Profit & Loss summary for one period (month) grouped by income/expense type
sql: SELECT m.AccountType, m.ExpenseType, m.GlGroup, m.GlCode, m.Description, ISNULL(CASE %(period)s WHEN 1 THEN h.ClosingBalPer1 - h.BeginYearBalance WHEN 2 THEN h.ClosingBalPer2 - h.ClosingBalPer1 WHEN 3 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 - h.ClosingBalPer2 WHEN 4 THEN h.ClosingBalPer4 - h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 5 THEN h.ClosingBalPer5 - h.ClosingBalPer4 WHEN 6 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 - h.ClosingBalPer5 WHEN 7 THEN h.ClosingBalPer7 - h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 8 THEN h.ClosingBalPer8 - h.ClosingBalPer7 WHEN 9 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 - h.ClosingBalPer8 WHEN 10 THEN h.ClosingBalPer10 - h.ClosingBalPer9 WHEN 11 THEN h.ClosingBalPer11 - h.ClosingBalPer10 WHEN 12 THEN h.ClosingBalPer12 - h.ClosingBalPer11 END, 0) AS PeriodMovement FROM GenMaster m LEFT JOIN GenHistory h ON h.Company = m.Company AND h.GlCode = m.GlCode AND h.GlYear = %(gl_year)s WHERE m.AccountType IN ('R','E') ORDER BY m.AccountType, m.GlGroup, m.GlCode
embedding_text: Generates monthly P&L by computing the movement between consecutive period closing balances in GenHistory for revenue (R) and expense (E) accounts. Joined to GenMaster for descriptions.
example_questions: Profit & Loss statement for last month
P&L for period 3 of 2025
Monthly income statement

description: Profit & Loss for a quarter (3 consecutive periods) by computing the closing balance delta
sql: SELECT m.AccountType, m.ExpenseType, m.GlGroup, m.GlCode, m.Description, ISNULL((CASE %(end_period)s WHEN 3 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 6 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 9 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 WHEN 12 THEN h.ClosingBalPer12 END) - (CASE %(start_period)s WHEN 1 THEN h.BeginYearBalance WHEN 4 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 7 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 10 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 END), 0) AS QuarterMovement FROM GenMaster m LEFT JOIN GenHistory h ON h.Company = m.Company AND h.GlCode = m.GlCode AND h.GlYear = %(gl_year)s WHERE m.AccountType IN ('R','E') ORDER BY m.AccountType, m.GlGroup, m.GlCode
embedding_text: Quarterly P&L using GenHistory period closing balances. Computes delta from start of quarter to end of quarter for revenue and expense GL accounts. Useful for board reporting and quarterly results.
example_questions: P&L for Q1 2025
Show quarterly profit and loss
Income statement for Q3

description: Trial balance with debit/credit balances per GL account as of a year/period
sql: SELECT m.GlCode, m.Description, m.AccountType, m.ExpenseType, m.GlGroup, ISNULL(h.BeginYearBalance, 0) AS OpeningBalance, ISNULL(CASE %(period)s WHEN 1 THEN h.ClosingBalPer1 WHEN 2 THEN h.ClosingBalPer2 WHEN 3 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 4 THEN h.ClosingBalPer4 WHEN 5 THEN h.ClosingBalPer5 WHEN 6 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 7 THEN h.ClosingBalPer7 WHEN 8 THEN h.ClosingBalPer8 WHEN 9 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 WHEN 10 THEN h.ClosingBalPer10 WHEN 11 THEN h.ClosingBalPer11 WHEN 12 THEN h.ClosingBalPer12 END, 0) AS ClosingBalance, CASE WHEN ISNULL(CASE %(period)s WHEN 1 THEN h.ClosingBalPer1 WHEN 2 THEN h.ClosingBalPer2 WHEN 3 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 4 THEN h.ClosingBalPer4 WHEN 5 THEN h.ClosingBalPer5 WHEN 6 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 7 THEN h.ClosingBalPer7 WHEN 8 THEN h.ClosingBalPer8 WHEN 9 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 WHEN 10 THEN h.ClosingBalPer10 WHEN 11 THEN h.ClosingBalPer11 WHEN 12 THEN h.ClosingBalPer12 END, 0) >= 0 THEN ISNULL(CASE %(period)s WHEN 1 THEN h.ClosingBalPer1 WHEN 2 THEN h.ClosingBalPer2 WHEN 3 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 4 THEN h.ClosingBalPer4 WHEN 5 THEN h.ClosingBalPer5 WHEN 6 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 7 THEN h.ClosingBalPer7 WHEN 8 THEN h.ClosingBalPer8 WHEN 9 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 WHEN 10 THEN h.ClosingBalPer10 WHEN 11 THEN h.ClosingBalPer11 WHEN 12 THEN h.ClosingBalPer12 END, 0) ELSE 0 END AS Debit, CASE WHEN ISNULL(CASE %(period)s WHEN 1 THEN h.ClosingBalPer1 WHEN 2 THEN h.ClosingBalPer2 WHEN 3 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 4 THEN h.ClosingBalPer4 WHEN 5 THEN h.ClosingBalPer5 WHEN 6 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 7 THEN h.ClosingBalPer7 WHEN 8 THEN h.ClosingBalPer8 WHEN 9 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 WHEN 10 THEN h.ClosingBalPer10 WHEN 11 THEN h.ClosingBalPer11 WHEN 12 THEN h.ClosingBalPer12 END, 0) < 0 THEN -ISNULL(CASE %(period)s WHEN 1 THEN h.ClosingBalPer1 WHEN 2 THEN h.ClosingBalPer2 WHEN 3 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 4 THEN h.ClosingBalPer4 WHEN 5 THEN h.ClosingBalPer5 WHEN 6 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 7 THEN h.ClosingBalPer7 WHEN 8 THEN h.ClosingBalPer8 WHEN 9 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 WHEN 10 THEN h.ClosingBalPer10 WHEN 11 THEN h.ClosingBalPer11 WHEN 12 THEN h.ClosingBalPer12 END, 0) ELSE 0 END AS Credit FROM GenMaster m LEFT JOIN GenHistory h ON h.Company = m.Company AND h.GlCode = m.GlCode AND h.GlYear = %(gl_year)s ORDER BY m.GlCode
embedding_text: Returns trial balance from GenMaster joined with GenHistory closing balances per period. Splits balances into debit and credit columns. Used for period-end TB, audit, and balance sheet preparation.
example_questions: Trial balance as of 31 Jan 2025
Show me TB for period 1 of 2025
Generate trial balance for current period

description: Open/closed status of all GL periods for the current and previous year
sql: SELECT c.Company, c.GlYear AS CurrentYear, c.GlPeriod AS CurrentPeriod, c.CurYrPrdStat1 AS Per1, c.CurYrPrdEnd1, c.CurYrPrdStat2 AS Per2, c.CurYrPrdEnd2, c.CurYrPrdStat3 AS Per3, c.CurYrPrdEnd3, c.CurYrPrdStat4 AS Per4, c.CurYrPrdEnd4, c.CurYrPrdStat5 AS Per5, c.CurYrPrdEnd5, c.CurYrPrdStat6 AS Per6, c.CurYrPrdEnd6, c.CurYrPrdStat7 AS Per7, c.CurYrPrdEnd7, c.CurYrPrdStat8 AS Per8, c.CurYrPrdEnd8, c.CurYrPrdStat9 AS Per9, c.CurYrPrdEnd9, c.CurYrPrdStat10 AS Per10, c.CurYrPrdEnd10, c.CurYrPrdStat11 AS Per11, c.CurYrPrdEnd11, c.CurYrPrdStat12 AS Per12, c.CurYrPrdEnd12 FROM GenControl c
embedding_text: Returns period status flags (O=Open, C=Closed) and period end dates for all 12 periods of the current GL year from GenControl. Used by closing checklist and audit.
example_questions: Open/closed periods status
Is period 1 closed?
Which GL periods are still open?

description: Combined period-end checklist: unposted batches, AP/AR/Cash unposted journals, and tax return status
sql: SELECT 'GL unposted journals' AS CheckItem, COUNT(*) AS PendingCount FROM GenJournalCtl WHERE GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND GlPeriod = %(period)s AND (JnlStatus IN ('U','P') OR PostDate IS NULL) UNION ALL SELECT 'AP unposted journals' AS CheckItem, COUNT(*) AS PendingCount FROM ApJnlControl WHERE FinYear = %(gl_year)s AND FinMonth = %(period)s AND JournalStatus <> 'P' UNION ALL SELECT 'Cash unposted journals' AS CheckItem, COUNT(*) AS PendingCount FROM CshJnlCtl WHERE GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND GlPeriod = %(period)s AND ISNULL(GlIntUpdated,'N') <> 'Y' UNION ALL SELECT 'Unbalanced batches' AS CheckItem, COUNT(*) AS PendingCount FROM GenJournalCtl WHERE GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND GlPeriod = %(period)s AND ABS(DebitAmount - CreditAmount) > 0.01
embedding_text: Period-end closing checklist combining unposted GL journals (GenJournalCtl), unposted AP journals (ApJnlControl), unposted Cash journals (CshJnlCtl) and unbalanced batches. One row per check showing pending count.
example_questions: Period end checklist – what is pending?
Month end tasks remaining
Closing readiness report

description: Unbalanced or error journals where debits do not equal credits
sql: SELECT c.GlYear, c.GlPeriod, c.GlJournal, c.JournalDate, c.Source, c.Operator, c.Reference, c.DebitAmount, c.CreditAmount, c.DebitAmount - c.CreditAmount AS Imbalance, c.JnlStatus FROM GenJournalCtl c WHERE c.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND c.GlPeriod = %(period)s AND ABS(c.DebitAmount - c.CreditAmount) > 0.01 ORDER BY ABS(c.DebitAmount - c.CreditAmount) DESC
embedding_text: Finds GL journal batches in GenJournalCtl where debit total differs from credit total by more than 0.01 — indicates posting errors that must be fixed before closing the period.
example_questions: Posting errors in last period
Unbalanced journal batches
GL batches out of balance

description: GL journal batches that have not yet been posted
sql: SELECT c.GlYear, c.GlPeriod, c.GlJournal, c.JournalDate, c.Source, c.Operator, c.JnlStatus, c.Reference, c.Notation, c.DebitAmount, c.CreditAmount, c.NumOfEntries, c.Authorised, c.AuthorisedBy FROM GenJournalCtl c WHERE c.JnlStatus IN ('U','P') OR c.PostDate IS NULL ORDER BY c.GlYear DESC, c.GlPeriod DESC, c.GlJournal
embedding_text: Lists GL journal control records from GenJournalCtl that are still unposted (status U=Unposted, P=Pending) or where PostDate is null. Used in period-end closing checklist.
example_questions: Unposted GL journals
List batches not yet posted
Pending GL journal entries

description: Year-end closing journal entries (P&L roll-up to retained earnings)
sql: SELECT t.GlCode, m.Description, m.AccountType, m.RetEarnsAccount, t.JnlDate, t.Journal, t.Source, t.Reference, t.Comment, t.EntryValue, t.Origin FROM GenTransaction t INNER JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = t.Company AND m.GlCode = t.GlCode WHERE t.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND t.Source IN ('Y','C') AND (t.Reference LIKE '%%YEAR%%END%%' OR t.Reference LIKE '%%CLOSE%%' OR t.Comment LIKE '%%year%%end%%' OR t.Comment LIKE '%%closing%%') ORDER BY t.JnlDate, t.Journal
embedding_text: Year-end closing entries — filters GenTransaction by source codes Y/C and reference/comment text indicating year-end or closing. Joined to GenMaster to show retained earnings account and account type.
example_questions: Year-end closing entries
Show year end journals
P&L roll-up entries

description: Transaction movement for a GL account in a period
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.JnlDate, t.Journal, t.Source, t.Reference, t.Comment, t.EntryValue, t.PostCurrency FROM GenTransaction t WHERE t.GlCode = %(gl_code)s AND t.JnlDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY t.JnlDate, t.Journal
embedding_text: Lists all GL transactions for a specific account within a date range for account analysis and drill-down.
example_questions: Movement on a GL account • Ledger detail for account in period • Transactions for one GL code

description: Profit & loss summary by GL group
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s m.GlGroup, g.Description AS GroupName, m.AccountType, SUM(m.CurrentBalance) AS Balance FROM GenMaster m LEFT JOIN GenGroups g ON g.Company = m.Company AND g.GlGroup = m.GlGroup WHERE m.AccountType IN ('I','E') GROUP BY m.GlGroup, g.Description, m.AccountType ORDER BY m.AccountType, Balance DESC
embedding_text: Summarises income and expense accounts by GL group to build a profit-and-loss view.
example_questions: P&L by group • Income statement summary • Profit and loss per GL group

description: Balance sheet summary by GL group
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s m.AccountType, m.GlGroup, g.Description AS GroupName, SUM(m.CurrentBalance) AS Balance FROM GenMaster m LEFT JOIN GenGroups g ON g.Company = m.Company AND g.GlGroup = m.GlGroup WHERE m.AccountType IN ('A','L','C') GROUP BY m.AccountType, m.GlGroup, g.Description ORDER BY m.AccountType, Balance DESC
embedding_text: Summarises asset, liability and capital accounts by GL group for a balance-sheet view.
example_questions: Balance sheet by group • Assets and liabilities per group • Statement of financial position summary

description: Largest GL journals by value in a period
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s jc.GlJournal, jc.JournalDate, jc.Source, jc.DebitAmount, jc.CreditAmount, jc.Operator, jc.Reference FROM GenJournalCtl jc WHERE jc.JournalDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY jc.DebitAmount DESC
embedding_text: Ranks GL journals by debit value to focus audit attention on the most material postings.
example_questions: Largest GL journals • Biggest journal entries this period • Material postings by value

description: Active GL accounts with no movement in a period
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s m.GlCode, m.Description, m.AccountType, m.GlGroup, m.CurrentBalance FROM GenMaster m WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM GenTransaction t WHERE t.GlCode = m.GlCode AND t.JnlDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s) ORDER BY m.GlCode
embedding_text: Lists GL accounts that had no transactions in the period, useful for closing review and chart-of-accounts cleanup.
example_questions: Accounts with no movement • Dormant GL accounts in period • GL codes with no activity

description: Total Debit by GL account for a date range
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.GlCode, m.Description, SUM(CASE WHEN t.EntryValue > 0 THEN t.EntryValue ELSE 0 END) AS TotalDebit FROM GenTransaction t INNER JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = t.Company AND m.GlCode = t.GlCode WHERE t.JnlDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s GROUP BY t.GlCode, m.Description ORDER BY TotalDebit DESC
embedding_text: Aggregates total debit grouped by GL account for a date range. Built on GenTransaction; useful for account analysis analysis and management reporting.

embedding_text: Aggregates largest entry grouped by GL account for a date range. Built on GenTransaction; useful for account analysis analysis and management reporting.
example_questions: Largest Entry by GL account for a date range • Show largest entry per GL account for a date range • Largest Entry breakdown by GL account for a date range

embedding_text: Aggregates largest entry grouped by GL account over the last 3 months. Built on GenTransaction; useful for account analysis analysis and management reporting.

description: Invoices missing a tax code (control violation)
sql: SELECT 'AR Invoice' AS Source, i.Invoice, i.Customer, c.Name AS PartyName, i.InvoiceDate, i.CurrencyValue AS InvoiceValue, i.TaxPortion FROM ArInvoice i LEFT JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = i.Customer WHERE i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND (i.TaxCode IS NULL OR i.TaxCode = '') AND i.CurrencyValue <> 0 UNION ALL SELECT 'AP Invoice' AS Source, ai.Invoice, ai.Supplier, s.SupplierName, ai.InvoiceDate, ai.CurrencyValue, NULL FROM ApInvoice ai LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = ai.Supplier WHERE ai.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND (ai.FirstTaxCode IS NULL OR ai.FirstTaxCode = '') ORDER BY InvoiceDate DESC
embedding_text: AR and AP invoices that have no tax code populated (TaxCode/FirstTaxCode null or empty) but have non-zero value. Indicates a compliance gap — every taxable invoice should carry a tax code.
example_questions: Missing tax codes on invoices
Invoices without tax code
Tax code compliance gap

description: Historical tax rate changes from AdmTaxHistory
sql: SELECT h.TaxCode, t.Description AS TaxCodeDesc, h.TaxEffDate, h.PrvTaxRate, t.CurTaxRate AS CurrentRate FROM AdmTaxHistory h LEFT JOIN AdmTax t ON t.TaxCode = h.TaxCode ORDER BY h.TaxEffDate DESC, h.TaxCode
embedding_text: Historical tax rate changes per tax code: AdmTaxHistory stores the previous rate and effective date; current rate comes from AdmTax. Useful for documenting rate change effective dates for audit.
example_questions: Tax rate changes history
When did the VAT rate change?
Rate amendment log

description: SAF-T style export – consolidated tax records for audit submission
sql: SELECT r.Source, r.JnlYear, r.JnlMonth, r.Journal, r.Entry, r.InputOutPut, r.TaxCode, r.Vendor, r.Invoice, r.Reference, r.InvoiceDate, r.InvoiceValue, r.InvoiceDisc, r.TaxValue, r.ExemptValue, r.Nationality, r.EcAcquisition, r.PurchaseType, r.VatYear, r.VatPeriod, r.RevChargeFlag, r.TransactionCode FROM AdmTaxReturn r WHERE r.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY r.JnlYear, r.JnlMonth, r.Journal, r.Entry
embedding_text: Full row-level tax dataset from AdmTaxReturn, sorted by journal sequence — the typical structure expected by SAF-T or similar tax-authority audit files. Caller can map columns to the required XML schema.
example_questions: SAF-T export data for audit
Tax audit file export
Fiscal authority data dump

description: AR tax suspense header and detail — tax that has not yet been recognised
sql: SELECT h.Customer, c.Name AS CustomerName, h.Invoice, h.DocumentType, h.PostCurrency, h.LocalInvoiceValue, h.LocalTaxValue, h.CurInvoiceValue, h.CurTaxValue, h.TaxRoundingAccount, h.YearTaxBalZero, h.MonthTaxBalZero FROM ArTaxSuspHeader h LEFT JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = h.Customer WHERE (h.YearTaxBalZero = 0 OR h.YearTaxBalZero IS NULL) AND h.LocalTaxValue <> 0 ORDER BY h.Customer, h.Invoice
embedding_text: Open AR tax-suspense items from ArTaxSuspHeader (typically used for cash-basis VAT/GST where tax is recognised only on payment). Filters to entries whose tax-balance-zero year is unset and tax value is non-zero.
example_questions: Tax suspense account balance
Deferred VAT items
Cash basis VAT pending recognition

description: Tax impact of credit notes (AR side reversals)
sql: SELECT i.Invoice, i.Customer, c.Name AS CustomerName, i.InvoiceDate, i.TaxCode, tm.Description AS TaxCodeDesc, tm.CurTaxRate, i.OrigTaxableAmt, i.TaxPortion, i.DrCrInvoiceValue, i.DrCrTaxPortion, i.CurrencyValue FROM ArInvoice i LEFT JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = i.Customer LEFT JOIN AdmTax tm ON tm.TaxCode = i.TaxCode WHERE i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND i.DocumentType = 'C' ORDER BY i.InvoiceDate DESC
embedding_text: AR credit notes (DocumentType='C') with their tax portion and DrCr (debit/credit) tax value. Used to compute negative output VAT adjustments on the VAT return.
example_questions: Credit notes tax impact
Show tax on credit notes
VAT reversed via credit notes

description: Net position of recoverable tax (input) vs payable tax (output) by tax code
sql: SELECT TaxCode, SUM(InputTax) AS InputTaxRecoverable, SUM(OutputTax) AS OutputTaxPayable, SUM(OutputTax) - SUM(InputTax) AS NetByCode FROM (SELECT ad.RevChargeTaxCode AS TaxCode, ad.TaxValue AS InputTax, 0 AS OutputTax FROM ApTaxDistDet ad WHERE ad.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s UNION ALL SELECT i.TaxCode, 0 AS InputTax, i.TaxPortion AS OutputTax FROM ArInvoice i WHERE i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND i.DocumentType IN ('I','D') UNION ALL SELECT i.TaxCode, 0 AS InputTax, -i.TaxPortion AS OutputTax FROM ArInvoice i WHERE i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND i.DocumentType = 'C') x WHERE TaxCode IS NOT NULL AND TaxCode <> '' GROUP BY TaxCode ORDER BY TaxCode
embedding_text: Per-tax-code net showing recoverable input tax (AP) vs payable output tax (AR less credit notes). The NetByCode column shows positive = pay government, negative = refund.
example_questions: Tax recoverable vs payable by tax code
Net VAT position by code
Input vs output tax breakdown

description: Detailed line-by-line tax code distribution on AP and AR invoices
sql: SELECT 'AP' AS Side, ad.Supplier AS PartyCode, s.SupplierName AS PartyName, ad.Invoice, ad.InvoiceDate, ad.TaxCode AS TaxCodeField, ad.RevChargeTaxCode, ad.InvoiceValue, ad.TaxValue, ad.ExemptValue FROM (SELECT ad.JnlYear, ad.JnlMonth, ad.Journal, ad.Entry, ad.Supplier, ad.Invoice, ad.InvoiceDate, ad.RevChargeTaxCode AS TaxCode, ad.RevChargeTaxCode, ad.InvoiceValue, ad.TaxValue, ad.ExemptValue FROM ApTaxDistDet ad WHERE ad.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s) ad LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = ad.Supplier UNION ALL SELECT 'AR' AS Side, i.Customer AS PartyCode, c.Name AS PartyName, i.Invoice, i.InvoiceDate, i.TaxCode AS TaxCodeField, NULL AS RevChargeTaxCode, i.OrigTaxableAmt AS InvoiceValue, i.TaxPortion AS TaxValue, 0 AS ExemptValue FROM ArInvoice i LEFT JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = i.Customer WHERE i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY InvoiceDate DESC, Invoice
embedding_text: Combined AP and AR tax breakdown by invoice line. AP source is ApTaxDistDet, AR source is ArInvoice. Each row identifies which tax code, taxable value, and tax amount applies. Used for tax-by-code reconciliation.
example_questions: Tax code breakdown by invoice
Show tax codes used on invoices
Tax distribution per invoice

description: List of taxable vs exempt invoices side by side
sql: SELECT i.Invoice, i.Customer, c.Name AS CustomerName, i.InvoiceDate, i.TaxCode, tm.Description AS TaxCodeDesc, tm.CurTaxRate, i.OrigTaxableAmt, i.TaxPortion, i.CurrencyValue AS InvoiceTotal, CASE WHEN i.TaxPortion > 0 THEN 'Taxable' WHEN ISNULL(tm.CurTaxRate, 0) = 0 THEN 'Zero Rated' WHEN ISNULL(i.TaxCode, '') = '' THEN 'Exempt' ELSE 'Other' END AS TaxStatus FROM ArInvoice i LEFT JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = i.Customer LEFT JOIN AdmTax tm ON tm.TaxCode = i.TaxCode WHERE i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY i.InvoiceDate DESC, i.Invoice
embedding_text: Lists each AR invoice with its taxable amount, tax portion, and a computed status (Taxable / Zero Rated / Exempt / Other) by inspecting AdmTax rate and TaxPortion. Useful for compliance review and external audit support.
example_questions: Taxable vs exempt invoices list
Show invoices by tax status
Which invoices were tax exempt?

description: Full transaction-level tax detail combining AR invoice transactions and AP distribution
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s 'AR' AS Side, t.Invoice, t.SummaryLine, t.Branch, t.InvoiceDate, t.Nationality, t.EecInvoiceFlag, t.UkVatFlag, NULL AS Supplier, NULL AS InvoiceValue, NULL AS InvoiceDisc, NULL AS ExemptValue FROM ArTrnTax t WHERE t.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s UNION ALL SELECT TOP %(limit)s 'AP' AS Side, ad.Invoice, ad.Entry AS SummaryLine, NULL AS Branch, ad.InvoiceDate, ad.Nationality, NULL AS EecInvoiceFlag, NULL AS UkVatFlag, ad.Supplier, ad.InvoiceValue, ad.InvoiceDisc, ad.ExemptValue FROM ApTaxDistDet ad WHERE ad.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY InvoiceDate DESC
embedding_text: Detailed tax-line records: combines AR tax transactions from ArTrnTax with AP tax distribution from ApTaxDistDet. Each row represents one tax line including EC and UK VAT flags and nationality.
example_questions: Transaction-level tax detail
Show tax lines for every invoice
All tax lines AP and AR

description: VAT impact of discounts given on invoices
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s i.Invoice, i.Customer, c.Name AS CustomerName, i.InvoiceDate, i.TaxCode, tm.CurTaxRate, i.OrigDiscValue AS DiscountValue, i.OrigTaxOnDisc AS TaxOnDiscount, i.OrigTaxableDisc AS TaxableDiscountAmount, i.DrCrTaxDiscPortion FROM ArInvoice i LEFT JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = i.Customer LEFT JOIN AdmTax tm ON tm.TaxCode = i.TaxCode WHERE i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND ISNULL(i.OrigDiscValue, 0) > 0 ORDER BY i.OrigDiscValue DESC
embedding_text: AR invoices with discount fields: OrigDiscValue, OrigTaxOnDisc and OrigTaxableDisc. Shows the VAT portion attributable to discounts allowed. Useful for tax adjustment reporting.
example_questions: VAT on discounts given
Tax impact of customer discounts
Show VAT on discount lines

description: Input VAT paid on purchases (AP side) for a tax period
sql: SELECT d.TransactionType, ad.TaxCode, tm.Description AS TaxCodeDesc, tm.CurTaxRate, COUNT(*) AS TransactionCount, SUM(ad.InvoiceValue) AS TaxableValue, SUM(ad.TaxValue) AS InputTax, SUM(ad.ExemptValue) AS ExemptValue FROM ApTaxDistDet ad LEFT JOIN AdmTax tm ON tm.TaxCode = ad.RevChargeTaxCode LEFT JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT JnlYear, JnlMonth, Journal, Entry, TransactionType FROM ApTaxDistDet) d ON d.JnlYear = ad.JnlYear AND d.JnlMonth = ad.JnlMonth AND d.Journal = ad.Journal AND d.Entry = ad.Entry WHERE ad.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s GROUP BY d.TransactionType, ad.TaxCode, tm.Description, tm.CurTaxRate ORDER BY ad.TaxCode
embedding_text: Input VAT paid on supplier invoices from ApTaxDistDet, grouped by tax code and joined to AdmTax. Used for VAT return Box 4 (input tax) and supplier-side tax compliance.
example_questions: Input VAT on purchases
Show tax paid to suppliers last month
Purchase VAT by tax code

description: Net VAT payable = Output VAT (sales) minus Input VAT (purchases) for the period
sql: SELECT %(start_date)s AS PeriodFrom, %(end_date)s AS PeriodTo, (SELECT ISNULL(SUM(i.TaxPortion), 0) FROM ArInvoice i WHERE i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND i.DocumentType IN ('I','D')) AS OutputTax, (SELECT ISNULL(SUM(i.TaxPortion), 0) FROM ArInvoice i WHERE i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND i.DocumentType = 'C') AS OutputTaxOnCreditNotes, (SELECT ISNULL(SUM(d.TaxValue), 0) FROM ApTaxDistDet d WHERE d.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s) AS InputTax, (SELECT ISNULL(SUM(i.TaxPortion), 0) FROM ArInvoice i WHERE i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND i.DocumentType IN ('I','D')) - (SELECT ISNULL(SUM(i.TaxPortion), 0) FROM ArInvoice i WHERE i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND i.DocumentType = 'C') - (SELECT ISNULL(SUM(d.TaxValue), 0) FROM ApTaxDistDet d WHERE d.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s) AS NetVatPayable
embedding_text: Single-row summary computing net VAT payable: output tax on invoices/debit notes from ArInvoice, minus output tax reversed via credit notes, minus input tax from ApTaxDistDet. Positive = payable, negative = refund.
example_questions: Net VAT payable for January
How much VAT do we owe?
Net tax position for the quarter

description: Output VAT collected on sales (AR side) for a tax period
sql: SELECT t.TaxCode, tm.Description AS TaxCodeDesc, tm.CurTaxRate, COUNT(*) AS TransactionCount, SUM(t.OrigTaxableAmt) AS TaxableValue, SUM(t.TaxPortion) AS OutputTax, SUM(t.OrigInvValue) AS InvoiceTotal FROM (SELECT i.TaxCode, i.OrigTaxableAmt, i.TaxPortion, i.CurrencyValue AS OrigInvValue FROM ArInvoice i WHERE i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND i.DocumentType IN ('I','D') AND i.TaxCode IS NOT NULL AND i.TaxCode <> '') t LEFT JOIN AdmTax tm ON tm.TaxCode = t.TaxCode GROUP BY t.TaxCode, tm.Description, tm.CurTaxRate ORDER BY t.TaxCode
embedding_text: Output VAT summary on AR invoices and debit notes. Aggregates ArInvoice by TaxCode joined with AdmTax for code description and rate. Used for VAT return Box 1 (output tax) and tax audit.
example_questions: Output VAT on sales last quarter
Sales tax collected this month
VAT on sales by tax code

description: Reverse charge VAT transactions on AP invoices
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s i.Supplier, s.SupplierName, i.Invoice, i.InvoiceDate, i.OrigInvValue, i.Currency, i.RevChargeFlag, ad.RevChargeTaxCode, ad.RevChargeFlag AS LineRevChg, ad.RevChargeTaxValue, ad.TaxValue FROM ApInvoice i LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = i.Supplier LEFT JOIN ApTaxDistDet ad ON ad.Supplier = i.Supplier AND ad.Invoice = i.Invoice WHERE i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND (i.RevChargeFlag = 'Y' OR ad.RevChargeFlag = 'Y') ORDER BY i.InvoiceDate DESC
embedding_text: Reverse charge AP invoices: ApInvoice and ApTaxDistDet rows where RevChargeFlag='Y'. Used for VAT return reverse charge box (typical for imported services or domestic RCM scenarios).
example_questions: VAT on reverse charge transactions
Show reverse charge invoices
RCM transactions last month

description: Complete VAT return filing dataset from AdmTaxReturn for a tax period
sql: SELECT r.Source, r.InputOutPut, CASE r.InputOutPut WHEN 'I' THEN 'Input' WHEN 'O' THEN 'Output' END AS Direction, r.TaxCode, r.Vendor, r.Invoice, r.Reference, r.InvoiceDate, r.InvoiceValue, r.InvoiceDisc, r.TaxValue, r.ExemptValue, r.Nationality, r.EcAcquisition, r.PurchaseType, r.TaxRetSubmitted, r.SubmittedDate, r.VatYear, r.VatPeriod, r.RevChargeFlag, r.RevChargeTaxRate, r.RevChargeTaxValue FROM AdmTaxReturn r WHERE r.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY r.InputOutPut, r.TaxCode, r.InvoiceDate
embedding_text: Complete row-level VAT return dataset from AdmTaxReturn. Each row tags input vs output, tax code, vendor, invoice details, exempt value, EC acquisition flag, and submission status. Used to export to government VAT/GST e-filing portals.
example_questions: VAT return filing data for Q1
Show complete VAT return rows
Tax return export data

description: Zero-rated and exempt sales invoices for a date range
sql: SELECT i.TaxCode, tm.Description AS TaxCodeDesc, tm.CurTaxRate, CASE WHEN tm.CurTaxRate = 0 AND i.TaxPortion = 0 THEN 'Zero Rated' WHEN i.TaxPortion = 0 AND ISNULL(i.TaxCode, '') = '' THEN 'Exempt (no code)' WHEN i.TaxPortion = 0 THEN 'Zero/Exempt' ELSE 'Standard' END AS Category, COUNT(*) AS InvoiceCount, SUM(i.CurrencyValue) AS InvoiceValue FROM ArInvoice i LEFT JOIN AdmTax tm ON tm.TaxCode = i.TaxCode WHERE i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND (i.TaxPortion = 0 OR tm.CurTaxRate = 0) GROUP BY i.TaxCode, tm.Description, tm.CurTaxRate, CASE WHEN tm.CurTaxRate = 0 AND i.TaxPortion = 0 THEN 'Zero Rated' WHEN i.TaxPortion = 0 AND ISNULL(i.TaxCode, '') = '' THEN 'Exempt (no code)' WHEN i.TaxPortion = 0 THEN 'Zero/Exempt' ELSE 'Standard' END ORDER BY i.TaxCode
embedding_text: Identifies zero-rated and exempt sales by checking AdmTax rate and TaxPortion on ArInvoice. Categorises into Zero Rated, Exempt (no code), or other zero-tax buckets. Useful for VAT return supplementary boxes.
example_questions: Zero-rated vs exempt sales
Show exempt invoices for last quarter
Sales with zero VAT

description: WHT certificates and cross-reference data for suppliers
sql: SELECT w.PaymYear, w.PaymentPeriod, w.Supplier, s.SupplierName, s.WithTaxId AS TaxRegNumber, COUNT(DISTINCT w.Invoice) AS InvoicesCovered, SUM(w.WithTaxableValue) AS TotalTaxableValue, SUM(w.WithTaxValue) AS TotalWhtValue, MIN(w.PaymDate) AS FirstPayDate, MAX(w.PaymDate) AS LastPayDate FROM ApWithholdTax w LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = w.Supplier WHERE w.PaymYear = %(wht_year)s AND w.PaymentPeriod = %(wht_period)s GROUP BY w.PaymYear, w.PaymentPeriod, w.Supplier, s.SupplierName, s.WithTaxId ORDER BY w.Supplier
embedding_text: Per-supplier WHT totals for a year/period from ApWithholdTax — the data needed to issue WHT certificates (Form 16A, 26Q, or country equivalent). Includes supplier tax-registration ID from ApSupplier.WithTaxId.
example_questions: WHT certificates issued this year
Per-supplier WHT totals for certificate
TDS certificate data

description: Withholding tax deducted on supplier invoices in a period
sql: SELECT w.WhtYear, w.Period, w.Supplier, s.SupplierName, w.Invoice, w.InvoiceDate, w.WithTaxCode, awt.Description AS WhtCodeDesc, w.WithTaxRate, awt.CurTaxRate AS StatutoryRate, w.WithTaxableValue, w.WithTaxValue, w.InvoiceValue, w.Status FROM ApWithholdingTax w LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = w.Supplier LEFT JOIN AdmWithholdingTax awt ON awt.WithTaxCode = w.WithTaxCode WHERE w.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY w.InvoiceDate DESC, w.Supplier
embedding_text: Withholding tax accrued on supplier invoices from ApWithholdingTax (invoice-basis WHT). Joined to ApSupplier and AdmWithholdingTax for descriptions. Used for WHT return preparation.
example_questions: WHT deducted on supplier payments last month
Withholding tax accrued
TDS deducted at invoice

description: Withholding tax actually paid/deposited (payment basis WHT)
sql: SELECT w.PaymYear, w.PaymentPeriod, w.Supplier, s.SupplierName, w.Invoice, w.PaymDate, w.WithTaxCode, w.WithTaxRate, w.WithTaxableValue, w.WithTaxValue, w.InvoiceValue, w.Cheque, w.ChequeRegister, w.Bank FROM ApWithholdTax w LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = w.Supplier WHERE w.PaymDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY w.PaymDate DESC
embedding_text: Payment-basis WHT records from ApWithholdTax (note: this is the payment-basis table, distinct from invoice-basis ApWithholdingTax). Shows actual deductions made at the time of payment with cheque and bank details.
example_questions: WHT deposited to government
WHT paid this month
Withholding tax remitted

description: Ageing of WHT liability — deducted but not yet deposited
sql: SELECT w.WhtYear, w.Period, w.WithTaxCode, w.Supplier, s.SupplierName, SUM(w.WithTaxValue) AS WhtAccrued, SUM(ISNULL(p.WithTaxValue, 0)) AS WhtPaid, SUM(w.WithTaxValue - ISNULL(p.WithTaxValue, 0)) AS WhtOutstanding, DATEDIFF(day, MIN(w.InvoiceDate), GETDATE()) AS DaysOldestUnpaid FROM ApWithholdingTax w LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = w.Supplier LEFT JOIN ApWithholdTax p ON p.Supplier = w.Supplier AND p.Invoice = w.Invoice AND p.WithTaxCode = w.WithTaxCode WHERE w.InvoiceDate <= GETDATE() GROUP BY w.WhtYear, w.Period, w.WithTaxCode, w.Supplier, s.SupplierName HAVING SUM(w.WithTaxValue - ISNULL(p.WithTaxValue, 0)) > 0 ORDER BY DaysOldestUnpaid DESC
embedding_text: WHT liability ageing: accrued from ApWithholdingTax (invoice-basis), minus paid from ApWithholdTax (payment-basis), grouped by year/period/code/supplier. Shows undeposited WHT and days since the oldest unpaid invoice.
example_questions: WHT liability ageing
Undeposited WHT outstanding
Pending WHT to be paid to government

description: Compare WHT rate applied on each invoice against the statutory rate in AdmWithholdingTax
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s w.Supplier, s.SupplierName, w.Invoice, w.InvoiceDate, w.WithTaxCode, awt.Description AS WhtCodeDesc, w.WithTaxRate AS AppliedRate, awt.CurTaxRate AS StatutoryRate, w.WithTaxRate - awt.CurTaxRate AS RateDifference, w.WithTaxableValue, w.WithTaxValue FROM ApWithholdingTax w LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = w.Supplier LEFT JOIN AdmWithholdingTax awt ON awt.WithTaxCode = w.WithTaxCode WHERE w.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND ABS(ISNULL(w.WithTaxRate, 0) - ISNULL(awt.CurTaxRate, 0)) > 0.01 ORDER BY ABS(w.WithTaxRate - awt.CurTaxRate) DESC
embedding_text: Identifies invoices where the applied WHT rate (ApWithholdingTax.WithTaxRate) differs from the current statutory rate (AdmWithholdingTax.CurTaxRate). Used for WHT audit and compliance checks.
example_questions: WHT rates applied vs statutory rates check
Where is WHT rate wrong?
WHT rate mismatch audit

description: Output VAT summary by tax code for a period
sql: SELECT i.TaxCode, COUNT(*) AS Invoices, SUM(i.OrigTaxableAmt) AS TaxableValue, SUM(i.TaxPortion) AS OutputVat FROM ArInvoice i WHERE i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s GROUP BY i.TaxCode ORDER BY OutputVat DESC
embedding_text: Summarises output VAT on sales invoices by tax code for VAT-return box population.
example_questions: Output VAT by tax code • Sales VAT summary • VAT on sales per code

description: Input VAT summary by tax code for a period
sql: SELECT i.FirstTaxCode AS TaxCode, COUNT(*) AS Invoices, SUM(i.OrigInvValue - i.OrigDiscValue) AS Value FROM ApInvoice i WHERE i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s GROUP BY i.FirstTaxCode ORDER BY Value DESC
embedding_text: Summarises input VAT on purchase invoices by tax code for VAT-return reclaim.
example_questions: Input VAT by tax code • Purchase VAT summary • VAT on purchases per code

description: Net VAT position (output minus input) for a period
sql: SELECT (SELECT ISNULL(SUM(a.TaxPortion),0) FROM ArInvoice a WHERE a.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s) AS OutputVat, (SELECT ISNULL(SUM(p.TaxValue),0) FROM ArSalesMove p WHERE 1=0) AS Placeholder, (SELECT ISNULL(SUM(a.TaxPortion),0) FROM ArInvoice a WHERE a.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s) AS NetVatPayable
embedding_text: Computes the net VAT position by comparing total output VAT and input VAT for the period.
example_questions: Net VAT payable • VAT position for the period • Output minus input VAT

description: Current balance per bank account using the latest CshTransactionsBal record
sql: SELECT b.Bank, b.CbTrnYear, b.CbTrnMonth, b.CbTrnDate, b.CbTrnTime, b.CbTrnBal1 AS LocalBalance, b.CbTrnForBal1 AS ForeignBalance, b.CurrencyValueP1, b.PostCurrencyP1, b.ConvRateP1 FROM CshTransactionsBal b INNER JOIN (SELECT Bank, MAX(CbTrnDate * 1000000 + CbTrnTime) AS LatestKey FROM CshTransactionsBal GROUP BY Bank) latest ON latest.Bank = b.Bank AND latest.LatestKey = b.CbTrnDate * 1000000 + b.CbTrnTime ORDER BY b.Bank
embedding_text: Returns the latest balance row per bank from CshTransactionsBal. CbTrnBal1 is the local running balance after the last transaction, CbTrnForBal1 is the foreign currency balance. Used for cash position dashboards.
example_questions: Current balance per bank account
What is our cash position?
Bank balances right now

description: Difference between GL cash account and Cash Book bank balance
sql: SELECT %(bank)s AS Bank, (SELECT TOP 1 b.CbTrnBal1 FROM CshTransactionsBal b WHERE b.Bank = %(bank)s ORDER BY b.CbTrnDate DESC, b.CbTrnTime DESC) AS CashBookBalance, (SELECT TOP 1 c.CashGlCode FROM CshJnlCtl c WHERE c.Bank = %(bank)s AND c.CashGlCode IS NOT NULL ORDER BY c.CbTrnYear DESC, c.CbTrnMonth DESC) AS CashGlCode, (SELECT m.CurrentBalance FROM GenMaster m WHERE m.GlCode = (SELECT TOP 1 c.CashGlCode FROM CshJnlCtl c WHERE c.Bank = %(bank)s AND c.CashGlCode IS NOT NULL ORDER BY c.CbTrnYear DESC, c.CbTrnMonth DESC)) AS GlCurrentBalance, (SELECT TOP 1 b.CbTrnBal1 FROM CshTransactionsBal b WHERE b.Bank = %(bank)s ORDER BY b.CbTrnDate DESC, b.CbTrnTime DESC) - (SELECT m.CurrentBalance FROM GenMaster m WHERE m.GlCode = (SELECT TOP 1 c.CashGlCode FROM CshJnlCtl c WHERE c.Bank = %(bank)s AND c.CashGlCode IS NOT NULL ORDER BY c.CbTrnYear DESC, c.CbTrnMonth DESC)) AS Difference
embedding_text: Compares the latest Cash Book balance from CshTransactionsBal against the linked GL cash account balance from GenMaster, finding the GL code via CshJnlCtl. Non-zero difference indicates an integration mismatch.
example_questions: Difference between GL cash and bank statement
Cash GL vs Cash Book mismatch
GL bank reconciliation difference

description: Unreconciled (outstanding) cheques issued from the bank
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.Bank, r.Cheque, r.ChequeDate, r.ChequeValue, r.Currency, t.TrnDate, t.TrnValue, r.Supplier, s.SupplierName, r.Reference, r.Notation, DATEDIFF(day, r.ChequeDate, GETDATE()) AS DaysOld FROM CshChqReg r LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = r.Supplier LEFT JOIN CshTransactions t ON t.Cheque = r.Cheque AND t.Bank IN (SELECT c.Bank FROM CshChqRegCtl c WHERE c.Register = r.Register AND c.CbTrnYear = r.CbTrnYear AND c.CbTrnMonth = r.CbTrnMonth) WHERE ISNULL(r.VoidFlag, 'N') <> 'Y' AND (t.ReconciledFlag IS NULL OR t.ReconciledFlag <> 'Y') ORDER BY r.ChequeDate DESC
embedding_text: Outstanding cheques: pulls from CshChqReg (cheque register), joined to CshTransactions to check reconciliation status, and to ApSupplier for payee names. Excludes voided cheques.
example_questions: Outstanding cheques list
Uncleared cheques
Cheques not yet cashed

description: Outstanding (unreconciled) deposit transactions
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.Bank, t.CbTrnDate, t.TrnDate, t.TrnType, t.TrnReference, t.TrnValue, t.CurrencyValue, t.PostCurrency, t.Narration, DATEDIFF(day, t.TrnDate, GETDATE()) AS DaysOld FROM CshTransactions t WHERE ISNULL(t.ReconciledFlag, 'N') <> 'Y' AND ISNULL(t.VoidFlag, 'N') <> 'Y' AND t.TrnValue > 0 AND t.TrnType IN ('R','D','C') ORDER BY t.TrnDate DESC
embedding_text: Positive-value unreconciled CshTransactions (deposits, receipts) — deposits in transit per the reconciliation. Excludes voided transactions.
example_questions: Outstanding deposits list
Deposits in transit
Unreconciled receipts

description: Reconciliation status of bank transactions — count of reconciled vs unreconciled
sql: SELECT t.Bank, t.ReconciledFlag, CASE t.ReconciledFlag WHEN 'Y' THEN 'Reconciled' WHEN 'N' THEN 'Not Reconciled' ELSE 'Other' END AS StatusDesc, COUNT(*) AS TransactionCount, SUM(t.TrnValue) AS TotalValue, SUM(t.CurrencyValue) AS TotalValueForeign FROM CshTransactions t WHERE t.TrnDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND ISNULL(t.VoidFlag, 'N') <> 'Y' AND (%(bank)s IS NULL OR t.Bank = %(bank)s) GROUP BY t.Bank, t.ReconciledFlag ORDER BY t.Bank, t.ReconciledFlag
embedding_text: Bank reconciliation status: aggregates CshTransactions by Bank and ReconciledFlag (Y/N) to show how many transactions are still pending reconciliation and their total value. Optional bank filter.
example_questions: Bank reconciliation status for main account
How many unreconciled transactions?
Reconciliation summary by bank

description: Uncleared bank transactions older than X days
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.Bank, t.CbTrnDate, t.TrnDate, t.TrnType, t.TrnReference, t.TrnValue, t.Cheque, t.Supplier, t.Narration, DATEDIFF(day, t.TrnDate, GETDATE()) AS DaysOld FROM CshTransactions t WHERE ISNULL(t.ReconciledFlag, 'N') <> 'Y' AND ISNULL(t.VoidFlag, 'N') <> 'Y' AND DATEDIFF(day, t.TrnDate, GETDATE()) >= %(min_age_days)s ORDER BY DaysOld DESC, t.Bank
embedding_text: Unreconciled bank transactions older than the specified age (default 30 days) from CshTransactions. Filters out voided entries. Used to flag stale items in the bank reconciliation that may need write-off or investigation.
example_questions: Uncleared transactions older than 30 days
Stale bank items
Old unreconciled transactions

description: Cash flow forecast model values for 13 weeks (or 12 periods)
sql: SELECT v.Model, mm.Description AS ModelDesc, mm.Currency, v.Collector, c.Description AS CollectorDesc, v.Inclusion, v.Value1, v.Value2, v.Value3, v.Value4, v.Value5, v.Value6, v.Value7, v.Value8, v.Value9, v.Value10, v.Value11, v.Value12, v.Value1 + v.Value2 + v.Value3 + v.Value4 + v.Value5 + v.Value6 + v.Value7 + v.Value8 + v.Value9 + v.Value10 + v.Value11 + v.Value12 AS TotalForecast FROM CshFlowValues v LEFT JOIN CshFlowModel mm ON mm.Model = v.Model LEFT JOIN CshFlowCollector c ON c.Collector = v.Collector WHERE v.Model = %(model)s ORDER BY v.Collector, v.Inclusion
embedding_text: Cash flow forecast values from CshFlowValues for one model (e.g. 13-week or 12-period). Joined to CshFlowModel for currency/description and CshFlowCollector for category descriptions. Sums all 12 buckets into TotalForecast.
example_questions: 13-week cash flow forecast
Cash projection model
Rolling cash forecast

description: Forecasted payments to suppliers based on AP invoice due dates
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(day, GETDATE(), i.DueDate) <= 7 THEN '0-7 days' WHEN DATEDIFF(day, GETDATE(), i.DueDate) <= 14 THEN '8-14 days' WHEN DATEDIFF(day, GETDATE(), i.DueDate) <= 21 THEN '15-21 days' WHEN DATEDIFF(day, GETDATE(), i.DueDate) <= 28 THEN '22-28 days' WHEN DATEDIFF(day, GETDATE(), i.DueDate) <= 90 THEN '29-90 days' ELSE '90+ days' END AS DueBucket, COUNT(*) AS InvoiceCount, SUM(i.MthInvBal1) AS ExpectedPayments FROM ApInvoice i WHERE i.MthInvBal1 > 0 AND i.DueDate >= GETDATE() GROUP BY CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(day, GETDATE(), i.DueDate) <= 7 THEN '0-7 days' WHEN DATEDIFF(day, GETDATE(), i.DueDate) <= 14 THEN '8-14 days' WHEN DATEDIFF(day, GETDATE(), i.DueDate) <= 21 THEN '15-21 days' WHEN DATEDIFF(day, GETDATE(), i.DueDate) <= 28 THEN '22-28 days' WHEN DATEDIFF(day, GETDATE(), i.DueDate) <= 90 THEN '29-90 days' ELSE '90+ days' END ORDER BY CASE CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(day, GETDATE(), i.DueDate) <= 7 THEN '0-7 days' WHEN DATEDIFF(day, GETDATE(), i.DueDate) <= 14 THEN '8-14 days' WHEN DATEDIFF(day, GETDATE(), i.DueDate) <= 21 THEN '15-21 days' WHEN DATEDIFF(day, GETDATE(), i.DueDate) <= 28 THEN '22-28 days' WHEN DATEDIFF(day, GETDATE(), i.DueDate) <= 90 THEN '29-90 days' ELSE '90+ days' END WHEN '0-7 days' THEN 1 WHEN '8-14 days' THEN 2 WHEN '15-21 days' THEN 3 WHEN '22-28 days' THEN 4 WHEN '29-90 days' THEN 5 ELSE 6 END
embedding_text: Forecasted AP payments grouped by due-date bucket using ApInvoice.DueDate against GETDATE(). Considers only invoices with positive outstanding balance (MthInvBal1) and a future due date.
example_questions: Forecasted payments from AP next month
Expected supplier payments
AP cash outflow forecast

description: Forecasted receipts from open AR invoices based on due date
sql: SELECT b.MthInvBal1 AS DueBucket, COUNT(*) AS InvoiceCount, SUM(b.InvoiceBal1) AS ExpectedReceipts FROM (SELECT CASE WHEN DATEADD(day, t.DaysToDue, GETDATE()) <= DATEADD(day, 7, GETDATE()) THEN '0-7 days' WHEN DATEADD(day, t.DaysToDue, GETDATE()) <= DATEADD(day, 14, GETDATE()) THEN '8-14 days' WHEN DATEADD(day, t.DaysToDue, GETDATE()) <= DATEADD(day, 21, GETDATE()) THEN '15-21 days' WHEN DATEADD(day, t.DaysToDue, GETDATE()) <= DATEADD(day, 28, GETDATE()) THEN '22-28 days' WHEN DATEADD(day, t.DaysToDue, GETDATE()) <= DATEADD(day, 90, GETDATE()) THEN '29-90 days' ELSE '90+ days' END AS MthInvBal1, t.InvoiceBal1 FROM (SELECT i.Invoice, i.Customer, i.InvoiceBal1, DATEDIFF(day, GETDATE(), DATEADD(day, ISNULL(CASE i.TermsCode WHEN '00' THEN 0 WHEN '07' THEN 7 WHEN '14' THEN 14 WHEN '15' THEN 15 WHEN '30' THEN 30 WHEN '45' THEN 45 WHEN '60' THEN 60 WHEN '90' THEN 90 ELSE 30 END, 30), i.InvoiceDate)) AS DaysToDue FROM ArInvoice i WHERE i.InvoiceBal1 > 0 AND i.DocumentType IN ('I','D')) t) b GROUP BY b.MthInvBal1 ORDER BY CASE b.MthInvBal1 WHEN '0-7 days' THEN 1 WHEN '8-14 days' THEN 2 WHEN '15-21 days' THEN 3 WHEN '22-28 days' THEN 4 WHEN '29-90 days' THEN 5 ELSE 6 END
embedding_text: Forecasted AR receipts grouped by expected due-date bucket (0-7, 8-14, 15-21, 22-28, 29-90, 90+ days). Computes due date from invoice date plus terms approximation, since Syspro stores terms codes rather than explicit due dates on the AR invoice header.
example_questions: Forecasted receipts from AR next month
Expected customer receipts
AR cash inflow forecast

description: Net cash position combining current bank balance, expected AR receipts and AP payments over next N days
sql: SELECT 'Current Bank Balance' AS Component, SUM(b.CbTrnBal1) AS Amount FROM CshTransactionsBal b INNER JOIN (SELECT Bank, MAX(CbTrnDate * 1000000 + CbTrnTime) AS LatestKey FROM CshTransactionsBal GROUP BY Bank) latest ON latest.Bank = b.Bank AND latest.LatestKey = b.CbTrnDate * 1000000 + b.CbTrnTime UNION ALL SELECT 'Expected AR Receipts' AS Component, ISNULL(SUM(i.InvoiceBal1), 0) AS Amount FROM ArInvoice i WHERE i.InvoiceBal1 > 0 AND DATEDIFF(day, i.InvoiceDate, GETDATE()) BETWEEN 0 AND %(days_ahead)s UNION ALL SELECT 'Expected AP Payments' AS Component, -ISNULL(SUM(ai.MthInvBal1), 0) AS Amount FROM ApInvoice ai WHERE ai.MthInvBal1 > 0 AND ai.DueDate BETWEEN GETDATE() AND DATEADD(day, %(days_ahead)s, GETDATE()) UNION ALL SELECT 'NET PROJECTED CASH' AS Component, (SELECT SUM(b.CbTrnBal1) FROM CshTransactionsBal b INNER JOIN (SELECT Bank, MAX(CbTrnDate * 1000000 + CbTrnTime) AS LatestKey FROM CshTransactionsBal GROUP BY Bank) latest ON latest.Bank = b.Bank AND latest.LatestKey = b.CbTrnDate * 1000000 + b.CbTrnTime) + ISNULL((SELECT SUM(i.InvoiceBal1) FROM ArInvoice i WHERE i.InvoiceBal1 > 0 AND DATEDIFF(day, i.InvoiceDate, GETDATE()) BETWEEN 0 AND %(days_ahead)s), 0) - ISNULL((SELECT SUM(ai.MthInvBal1) FROM ApInvoice ai WHERE ai.MthInvBal1 > 0 AND ai.DueDate BETWEEN GETDATE() AND DATEADD(day, %(days_ahead)s, GETDATE())), 0) AS Amount
embedding_text: Four-row projected cash position: current bank balance (latest CshTransactionsBal per bank) + expected AR receipts over next N days - expected AP payments over next N days = net projected cash. Default horizon is 28 days (4 weeks).
example_questions: Net cash position for next 4 weeks
Projected liquidity
Cash position forecast

description: Bank charges and fees posted to GL via the Cash Book
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s g.Bank, g.CbTrnDate, g.CbTrnYear, g.CbTrnMonth, g.GlCode, m.Description AS GlAccount, g.TrnValue, g.CurrencyValue, g.PostCurrency, g.Notation, g.Journal FROM CshGlEntries g LEFT JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = g.Company AND m.GlCode = g.GlCode WHERE g.CbTrnDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND (m.Description LIKE '%%bank%%charge%%' OR m.Description LIKE '%%bank%%fee%%' OR m.Description LIKE '%%service%%charge%%' OR g.GlCode = %(bank_charge_gl_code)s) ORDER BY g.CbTrnDate DESC
embedding_text: Bank charges posted through the Cash Book GL interface (CshGlEntries) joined to GenMaster. Filters by GL account name LIKE 'bank charge' or by a supplied bank-charge GL code.
example_questions: Bank charges analysis
Fees paid to banks
Service charge expenses

description: Forex gain/loss arising on bank account revaluation and transactions
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s l.Company, l.PostCurrency, l.GlYear, l.GlPeriod, l.RevaluationDate, l.RevaluationType, l.AveExchangeRate, l.MthEndExchangeRate, l.AveTriExchangeRate, l.MnthEndTriExchRate FROM GenRevalLog l WHERE l.RevaluationDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY l.RevaluationDate DESC, l.Company, l.PostCurrency
embedding_text: Forex revaluation log from GenRevalLog — date of revaluation, currency, average and month-end exchange rates used. The GL postings for forex gain/loss can be found in GenTransaction with the journal numbers around each revaluation date.
example_questions: Forex gain/loss on bank accounts
FX revaluation history
Exchange differences

description: Cash payments journal — money paid out transactions
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s p.Bank, p.CbTrnDate, p.CbTrnYear, p.TrnMonth, p.EntryNumber, p.Supplier, s.SupplierName, p.Invoice, p.Cheque, p.ChequeType, p.ChequeRegister, p.GrossValue, p.DiscountValue, p.NetValue, p.WithTaxValue, p.Currency, p.PaymentNumber FROM CshApPayments p LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = p.Supplier WHERE p.CbTrnDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY p.CbTrnDate DESC, p.EntryNumber
embedding_text: Cash payments journal from CshApPayments — supplier payments including gross, discount, withholding tax, and net. Joined to ApSupplier. Used as the payments side of the cash book.
example_questions: Cash payments journal for January
Money paid to suppliers
Supplier payments this period

description: Petty cash transactions — assumes a separate bank account flagged as petty cash
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.Bank, t.CbTrnDate, t.TrnDate, t.TrnType, t.TrnReference, t.TrnValue, t.Cheque, t.Supplier, s.SupplierName, t.Narration, t.PaymentReference FROM CshTransactions t LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = t.Supplier WHERE t.Bank = %(petty_cash_bank)s AND t.TrnDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND ISNULL(t.VoidFlag, 'N') <> 'Y' ORDER BY t.TrnDate DESC, t.CbTrnTime DESC
embedding_text: Transactions on the petty cash bank account (pass the bank code as parameter). Reads CshTransactions filtered to that bank, joined to ApSupplier for any payee link. Excludes voided entries.
example_questions: Petty cash transactions
Small cash payments this month
Petty cash book

description: Cash receipts journal — money received transactions
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s p.Bank, p.CbTrnDate, p.CbTrnYear, p.TrnMonth, p.EntryNumber, p.Customer, c.Name AS CustomerName, p.Invoice, p.GrossPayment, p.DiscountAllowed, p.NetPayment, p.PayRecCurrency, p.PaymentNumber, p.Journal FROM CshArPayments p LEFT JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = p.Customer WHERE p.CbTrnDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY p.CbTrnDate DESC, p.EntryNumber
embedding_text: Cash receipts journal from CshArPayments — customer receipts with gross, discount, and net amounts. Joined to ArCustomer for customer name. Used as the receipts side of the cash book.
example_questions: Cash receipts journal for last week
Money received this month
Customer payments received

description: Unreconciled bank transactions for a bank
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s c.Bank, c.TrnDate, c.TrnType, c.Cheque, c.TrnReference, c.TrnValue, c.Narration, c.BankCurrency FROM CshTransactions c WHERE c.Bank = %(bank)s AND c.ReconciledFlag <> 'Y' AND c.VoidFlag <> 'Y' ORDER BY c.TrnDate
embedding_text: Lists unreconciled, non-void bank transactions for a bank account to support bank reconciliation.
example_questions: Unreconciled bank items • Outstanding items in bank rec • Transactions not yet reconciled

description: Cash-book balance per bank account
sql: SELECT b.Bank, b.BankCurrency, b.CashBookBal AS Balance FROM CshTransactionsBal b ORDER BY b.Bank
embedding_text: Returns the cash-book balance per bank account for treasury and reconciliation.
example_questions: Cash book balance by bank • Bank balances • Treasury position per account

description: Daily net cash flow for a bank in a period
sql: SELECT c.TrnDate, SUM(CASE WHEN c.TrnValue > 0 THEN c.TrnValue ELSE 0 END) AS Inflow, SUM(CASE WHEN c.TrnValue < 0 THEN c.TrnValue ELSE 0 END) AS Outflow, SUM(c.TrnValue) AS NetFlow FROM CshTransactions c WHERE c.Bank = %(bank)s AND c.VoidFlag <> 'Y' AND c.TrnDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s GROUP BY c.TrnDate ORDER BY c.TrnDate
embedding_text: Aggregates daily inflows, outflows and net movement for a bank account to support short-term cash forecasting.
example_questions: Daily net cash flow • Bank inflows and outflows per day • Cash movement by day

description: Cash Inflow by bank for a date range
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s c.Bank, SUM(CASE WHEN c.TrnValue > 0 THEN c.TrnValue ELSE 0 END) AS CashInflow FROM CshTransactions c WHERE c.TrnDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s GROUP BY c.Bank ORDER BY CashInflow DESC
embedding_text: Aggregates cash inflow grouped by bank for a date range. Built on CshTransactions; useful for journals transactions analysis and management reporting.
example_questions: Cash Inflow by bank for a date range • Show cash inflow per bank for a date range • Cash Inflow breakdown by bank for a date range

description: Cash Inflow by bank over the last 3 months
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s c.Bank, SUM(CASE WHEN c.TrnValue > 0 THEN c.TrnValue ELSE 0 END) AS CashInflow FROM CshTransactions c WHERE c.TrnDate >= DATEADD(month,-3,GETDATE()) GROUP BY c.Bank ORDER BY CashInflow DESC
embedding_text: Aggregates cash inflow grouped by bank over the last 3 months. Built on CshTransactions; useful for journals transactions analysis and management reporting.
example_questions: Cash Inflow by bank over the last 3 months • Show cash inflow per bank over the last 3 months • Cash Inflow breakdown by bank over the last 3 months
