description: Budget vs actual at cost-centre (analysis section) level
sql: SELECT s.SectionNumber, s.SectionCode, s.Description AS CostCentreName, s.AccountType, b.BudgetType, b.Budget1+b.Budget2+b.Budget3+b.Budget4+b.Budget5+b.Budget6+b.Budget7+b.Budget8+b.Budget9+b.Budget10+b.Budget11+b.Budget12 AS BudgetYearTotal, (SELECT ISNULL(SUM(t.EntryValue), 0) FROM GenTransaction t WHERE t.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM GenAnalysisSection a WHERE a.SectionNumber = s.SectionNumber AND a.SectionCode = s.SectionCode AND a.AnalysisEntry = t.AnalysisEntry)) AS ActualYearTotal FROM GenSection s LEFT JOIN GenAnalysisBudget b ON b.SectionNumber = s.SectionNumber AND b.SectionCode = s.SectionCode AND b.AccountType = s.AccountType WHERE (%(section_code)s IS NULL OR s.SectionCode = %(section_code)s) ORDER BY s.SectionNumber, s.SectionCode
embedding_text: Cost-centre budget vs actual: budget total from GenAnalysisBudget per section, actual total computed by summing GenTransaction EntryValue where the AnalysisEntry maps to that section via GenAnalysisSection. Joined to GenSection for descriptions.
example_questions: Budget vs actual by cost centre
Department budget performance
Cost centre over budget?

description: P&L budget vs actual variance with absolute and percentage variance
sql: SELECT m.GlCode, m.Description, m.AccountType, m.GlGroup, b.Budget1+b.Budget2+b.Budget3+b.Budget4+b.Budget5+b.Budget6+b.Budget7+b.Budget8+b.Budget9+b.Budget10+b.Budget11+b.Budget12 AS BudgetYTD, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer12 - h.BeginYearBalance, 0) AS ActualYTD, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer12 - h.BeginYearBalance, 0) - (b.Budget1+b.Budget2+b.Budget3+b.Budget4+b.Budget5+b.Budget6+b.Budget7+b.Budget8+b.Budget9+b.Budget10+b.Budget11+b.Budget12) AS Variance, CASE WHEN (b.Budget1+b.Budget2+b.Budget3+b.Budget4+b.Budget5+b.Budget6+b.Budget7+b.Budget8+b.Budget9+b.Budget10+b.Budget11+b.Budget12) <> 0 THEN 100.0 * (ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer12 - h.BeginYearBalance, 0) - (b.Budget1+b.Budget2+b.Budget3+b.Budget4+b.Budget5+b.Budget6+b.Budget7+b.Budget8+b.Budget9+b.Budget10+b.Budget11+b.Budget12)) / NULLIF((b.Budget1+b.Budget2+b.Budget3+b.Budget4+b.Budget5+b.Budget6+b.Budget7+b.Budget8+b.Budget9+b.Budget10+b.Budget11+b.Budget12), 0) ELSE NULL END AS VariancePct FROM GenMaster m INNER JOIN GenBudgets b ON b.Company = m.Company AND b.GlCode = m.GlCode AND b.BudgetType = %(budget_type)s AND b.BudgetNumber = %(budget_number)s LEFT JOIN GenHistory h ON h.Company = m.Company AND h.GlCode = m.GlCode AND h.GlYear = %(gl_year)s WHERE m.AccountType IN ('R','E') ORDER BY ABS(ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer12 - h.BeginYearBalance, 0) - (b.Budget1+b.Budget2+b.Budget3+b.Budget4+b.Budget5+b.Budget6+b.Budget7+b.Budget8+b.Budget9+b.Budget10+b.Budget11+b.Budget12)) DESC
embedding_text: Full-year P&L variance: budget from GenBudgets (sum of 12 periods), actuals from GenHistory (Per12 minus opening), absolute variance and variance percentage. Sorted by largest absolute variance first.
example_questions: P&L budget vs actual variance
Show me biggest variances
Where am I over/under budget?

description: Prior year actual as a budget benchmark — current year actual vs prior year actual
sql: SELECT m.GlCode, m.Description, m.AccountType, m.GlGroup, ISNULL(cy.ClosingBalPer12 - cy.BeginYearBalance, 0) AS CurrentYearActual, ISNULL(py.ClosingBalPer12 - py.BeginYearBalance, 0) AS PriorYearActual, ISNULL(cy.ClosingBalPer12 - cy.BeginYearBalance, 0) - ISNULL(py.ClosingBalPer12 - py.BeginYearBalance, 0) AS YoYChange, CASE WHEN ISNULL(py.ClosingBalPer12 - py.BeginYearBalance, 0) <> 0 THEN 100.0 * (ISNULL(cy.ClosingBalPer12 - cy.BeginYearBalance, 0) - ISNULL(py.ClosingBalPer12 - py.BeginYearBalance, 0)) / NULLIF(ISNULL(py.ClosingBalPer12 - py.BeginYearBalance, 0), 0) ELSE NULL END AS YoYPct FROM GenMaster m LEFT JOIN GenHistory cy ON cy.Company = m.Company AND cy.GlCode = m.GlCode AND cy.GlYear = %(current_year)s LEFT JOIN GenHistory py ON py.Company = m.Company AND py.GlCode = m.GlCode AND py.GlYear = %(prior_year)s WHERE m.AccountType IN ('R','E') ORDER BY ABS(ISNULL(cy.ClosingBalPer12 - cy.BeginYearBalance, 0) - ISNULL(py.ClosingBalPer12 - py.BeginYearBalance, 0)) DESC
embedding_text: Year-over-year actual comparison: current year P&L movement (GenHistory) vs prior year P&L movement for the same GL accounts. Useful when no formal budget exists and prior year acts as the benchmark.
example_questions: Prior year as budget comparison
YoY P&L comparison
How did this year compare to last?

description: Rolling forecast – actuals year-to-date plus budget for remaining periods
sql: SELECT m.GlCode, m.Description, m.AccountType, %(actual_through_period)s AS ActualsThroughPeriod, ISNULL((CASE %(actual_through_period)s WHEN 1 THEN h.ClosingBalPer1 WHEN 2 THEN h.ClosingBalPer2 WHEN 3 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 4 THEN h.ClosingBalPer4 WHEN 5 THEN h.ClosingBalPer5 WHEN 6 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 7 THEN h.ClosingBalPer7 WHEN 8 THEN h.ClosingBalPer8 WHEN 9 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 WHEN 10 THEN h.ClosingBalPer10 WHEN 11 THEN h.ClosingBalPer11 WHEN 12 THEN h.ClosingBalPer12 END) - h.BeginYearBalance, 0) AS ActualYTD, (CASE WHEN %(actual_through_period)s < 1 THEN b.Budget1 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN %(actual_through_period)s < 2 THEN b.Budget2 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN %(actual_through_period)s < 3 THEN b.Budget3 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN %(actual_through_period)s < 4 THEN b.Budget4 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN %(actual_through_period)s < 5 THEN b.Budget5 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN %(actual_through_period)s < 6 THEN b.Budget6 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN %(actual_through_period)s < 7 THEN b.Budget7 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN %(actual_through_period)s < 8 THEN b.Budget8 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN %(actual_through_period)s < 9 THEN b.Budget9 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN %(actual_through_period)s < 10 THEN b.Budget10 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN %(actual_through_period)s < 11 THEN b.Budget11 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN %(actual_through_period)s < 12 THEN b.Budget12 ELSE 0 END) AS BudgetRemainingPeriods, ISNULL((CASE %(actual_through_period)s WHEN 1 THEN h.ClosingBalPer1 WHEN 2 THEN h.ClosingBalPer2 WHEN 3 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 4 THEN h.ClosingBalPer4 WHEN 5 THEN h.ClosingBalPer5 WHEN 6 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 7 THEN h.ClosingBalPer7 WHEN 8 THEN h.ClosingBalPer8 WHEN 9 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 WHEN 10 THEN h.ClosingBalPer10 WHEN 11 THEN h.ClosingBalPer11 WHEN 12 THEN h.ClosingBalPer12 END) - h.BeginYearBalance, 0) + (CASE WHEN %(actual_through_period)s < 1 THEN b.Budget1 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN %(actual_through_period)s < 2 THEN b.Budget2 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN %(actual_through_period)s < 3 THEN b.Budget3 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN %(actual_through_period)s < 4 THEN b.Budget4 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN %(actual_through_period)s < 5 THEN b.Budget5 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN %(actual_through_period)s < 6 THEN b.Budget6 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN %(actual_through_period)s < 7 THEN b.Budget7 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN %(actual_through_period)s < 8 THEN b.Budget8 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN %(actual_through_period)s < 9 THEN b.Budget9 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN %(actual_through_period)s < 10 THEN b.Budget10 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN %(actual_through_period)s < 11 THEN b.Budget11 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN %(actual_through_period)s < 12 THEN b.Budget12 ELSE 0 END) AS RollingForecast FROM GenMaster m INNER JOIN GenBudgets b ON b.Company = m.Company AND b.GlCode = m.GlCode AND b.BudgetType = %(budget_type)s AND b.BudgetNumber = %(budget_number)s LEFT JOIN GenHistory h ON h.Company = m.Company AND h.GlCode = m.GlCode AND h.GlYear = %(gl_year)s WHERE m.AccountType IN ('R','E') ORDER BY m.AccountType, m.GlCode
embedding_text: Rolling forecast: actuals YTD from GenHistory through the specified period plus budget from GenBudgets for the remaining periods of the year. Produces a forecast that updates as actuals are booked.
example_questions: Rolling forecast for next 12 months
YTD actuals plus rest-of-year budget
Forecast remaining year

description: History of budget revisions / uploads
sql: SELECT b.Company, b.GlCode, m.Description, b.BudgetType, b.BudgetNumber, b.RevisionLevel, b.DateLastModified, b.Budget1+b.Budget2+b.Budget3+b.Budget4+b.Budget5+b.Budget6+b.Budget7+b.Budget8+b.Budget9+b.Budget10+b.Budget11+b.Budget12 AS YearTotal FROM GenBudgets b LEFT JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = b.Company AND m.GlCode = b.GlCode WHERE b.DateLastModified BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY b.DateLastModified DESC
embedding_text: Budget records with their last modification timestamp from GenBudgets. Shows budget type, number, revision level, and the year-total amount. Used to track when budgets were revised or uploaded.
example_questions: Budget upload history
When was the budget last revised?
Budget revision log

description: Variance Pct by GL account for the full year
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s m.GlCode, m.Description, m.AccountType, b.Budget1+b.Budget2+b.Budget3+b.Budget4+b.Budget5+b.Budget6+b.Budget7+b.Budget8+b.Budget9+b.Budget10+b.Budget11+b.Budget12 AS Budget, ISNULL((SELECT SUM(t.EntryValue) FROM GenTransaction t WHERE t.Company = m.Company AND t.GlCode = m.GlCode AND t.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND t.GlPeriod BETWEEN 1 AND 12),0) AS Actual, CASE WHEN (b.Budget1+b.Budget2+b.Budget3+b.Budget4+b.Budget5+b.Budget6+b.Budget7+b.Budget8+b.Budget9+b.Budget10+b.Budget11+b.Budget12) = 0 THEN 0 ELSE 100.0 * (ISNULL((SELECT SUM(t.EntryValue) FROM GenTransaction t WHERE t.Company = m.Company AND t.GlCode = m.GlCode AND t.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND t.GlPeriod BETWEEN 1 AND 12),0) - (b.Budget1+b.Budget2+b.Budget3+b.Budget4+b.Budget5+b.Budget6+b.Budget7+b.Budget8+b.Budget9+b.Budget10+b.Budget11+b.Budget12)) / (b.Budget1+b.Budget2+b.Budget3+b.Budget4+b.Budget5+b.Budget6+b.Budget7+b.Budget8+b.Budget9+b.Budget10+b.Budget11+b.Budget12) END AS VariancePct FROM GenBudgets b INNER JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = b.Company AND m.GlCode = b.GlCode WHERE b.BudgetType = %(budget_type)s ORDER BY VariancePct DESC
embedding_text: Compares budget and actual (variance pct) by GL account for the full year, with actuals drawn from GenTransaction for the matching periods.
example_questions: Variance Pct by GL account for the full year • Budget vs actual by GL account for the full year • Variance Pct report by GL account

sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s m.ExpenseType AS ExpenseType, SUM(b.Budget7+b.Budget8+b.Budget9) AS Budget, SUM(ISNULL((SELECT SUM(t.EntryValue) FROM GenTransaction t WHERE t.Company = m.Company AND t.GlCode = m.GlCode AND t.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND t.GlPeriod BETWEEN 7 AND 9),0)) AS Actual, SUM(ISNULL((SELECT SUM(t.EntryValue) FROM GenTransaction t WHERE t.Company = m.Company AND t.GlCode = m.GlCode AND t.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND t.GlPeriod BETWEEN 7 AND 9),0)) - SUM(b.Budget7+b.Budget8+b.Budget9) AS Variance FROM GenBudgets b INNER JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = b.Company AND m.GlCode = b.GlCode WHERE b.BudgetType = %(budget_type)s GROUP BY m.ExpenseType ORDER BY Variance DESC

description: Variance Pct by GL account for quarter 4
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s m.GlCode, m.Description, m.AccountType, b.Budget10+b.Budget11+b.Budget12 AS Budget, ISNULL((SELECT SUM(t.EntryValue) FROM GenTransaction t WHERE t.Company = m.Company AND t.GlCode = m.GlCode AND t.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND t.GlPeriod BETWEEN 10 AND 12),0) AS Actual, CASE WHEN (b.Budget10+b.Budget11+b.Budget12) = 0 THEN 0 ELSE 100.0 * (ISNULL((SELECT SUM(t.EntryValue) FROM GenTransaction t WHERE t.Company = m.Company AND t.GlCode = m.GlCode AND t.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND t.GlPeriod BETWEEN 10 AND 12),0) - (b.Budget10+b.Budget11+b.Budget12)) / (b.Budget10+b.Budget11+b.Budget12) END AS VariancePct FROM GenBudgets b INNER JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = b.Company AND m.GlCode = b.GlCode WHERE b.BudgetType = %(budget_type)s ORDER BY VariancePct DESC

description: Utilisation Pct by GL account for the full year
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s m.GlCode, m.Description, m.AccountType, b.Budget1+b.Budget2+b.Budget3+b.Budget4+b.Budget5+b.Budget6+b.Budget7+b.Budget8+b.Budget9+b.Budget10+b.Budget11+b.Budget12 AS Budget, ISNULL((SELECT SUM(t.EntryValue) FROM GenTransaction t WHERE t.Company = m.Company AND t.GlCode = m.GlCode AND t.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND t.GlPeriod BETWEEN 1 AND 12),0) AS Actual, CASE WHEN (b.Budget1+b.Budget2+b.Budget3+b.Budget4+b.Budget5+b.Budget6+b.Budget7+b.Budget8+b.Budget9+b.Budget10+b.Budget11+b.Budget12) = 0 THEN 0 ELSE 100.0 * ISNULL((SELECT SUM(t.EntryValue) FROM GenTransaction t WHERE t.Company = m.Company AND t.GlCode = m.GlCode AND t.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND t.GlPeriod BETWEEN 1 AND 12),0) / (b.Budget1+b.Budget2+b.Budget3+b.Budget4+b.Budget5+b.Budget6+b.Budget7+b.Budget8+b.Budget9+b.Budget10+b.Budget11+b.Budget12) END AS UtilisationPct FROM GenBudgets b INNER JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = b.Company AND m.GlCode = b.GlCode WHERE b.BudgetType = %(budget_type)s ORDER BY UtilisationPct DESC

sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s m.ExpenseType AS ExpenseType, SUM(b.Budget4+b.Budget5+b.Budget6) AS Budget, SUM(ISNULL((SELECT SUM(t.EntryValue) FROM GenTransaction t WHERE t.Company = m.Company AND t.GlCode = m.GlCode AND t.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND t.GlPeriod BETWEEN 4 AND 6),0)) AS Actual, SUM(ISNULL((SELECT SUM(t.EntryValue) FROM GenTransaction t WHERE t.Company = m.Company AND t.GlCode = m.GlCode AND t.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND t.GlPeriod BETWEEN 4 AND 6),0)) - SUM(b.Budget4+b.Budget5+b.Budget6) AS Variance FROM GenBudgets b INNER JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = b.Company AND m.GlCode = b.GlCode WHERE b.BudgetType = %(budget_type)s GROUP BY m.ExpenseType ORDER BY Variance DESC
embedding_text: Compares budget and actual (utilisation pct) by expense type for quarter 2, with actuals drawn from GenTransaction for the matching periods.
example_questions: Utilisation Pct by expense type for quarter 2 • Budget vs actual by expense type for quarter 2 • Utilisation Pct report by expense type

description: Utilisation Pct by retained-earnings account for quarter 2
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s m.RetEarnsAccount AS RetEarnsAccount, SUM(b.Budget4+b.Budget5+b.Budget6) AS Budget, SUM(ISNULL((SELECT SUM(t.EntryValue) FROM GenTransaction t WHERE t.Company = m.Company AND t.GlCode = m.GlCode AND t.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND t.GlPeriod BETWEEN 4 AND 6),0)) AS Actual, SUM(ISNULL((SELECT SUM(t.EntryValue) FROM GenTransaction t WHERE t.Company = m.Company AND t.GlCode = m.GlCode AND t.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND t.GlPeriod BETWEEN 4 AND 6),0)) - SUM(b.Budget4+b.Budget5+b.Budget6) AS Variance FROM GenBudgets b INNER JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = b.Company AND m.GlCode = b.GlCode WHERE b.BudgetType = %(budget_type)s GROUP BY m.RetEarnsAccount ORDER BY Variance DESC
embedding_text: Compares budget and actual (utilisation pct) by retained-earnings account for quarter 2, with actuals drawn from GenTransaction for the matching periods.
example_questions: Utilisation Pct by retained-earnings account for quarter 2 • Budget vs actual by retained-earnings account for quarter 2 • Utilisation Pct report by retained-earnings account

description: Asset account additions during a period — debit postings to asset GL accounts (no dedicated Asset module)
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.GlCode, m.Description AS AssetAccount, m.GlGroup, t.JnlDate, t.Journal, t.Source, t.Reference, t.Comment, t.EntryValue, t.PostCurrency, t.SubModRef, t.SubModSupplier, t.SubModApInvoice FROM GenTransaction t INNER JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = t.Company AND m.GlCode = t.GlCode WHERE m.AccountType = 'A' AND (m.GlGroup LIKE %(asset_group_pattern)s OR m.Description LIKE %(asset_desc_pattern)s) AND t.JnlDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND t.EntryValue > 0 ORDER BY t.JnlDate DESC, t.EntryValue DESC
embedding_text: Approximation of fixed-asset additions: debit postings (EntryValue > 0) to asset GL accounts within a date range from GenTransaction. Shows the source, supplier, and AP invoice link via SubMod* fields so each addition can be traced to its origin.
example_questions: Assets purchased this period
Capex additions
Fixed asset additions

description: List of fixed-asset GL accounts (the Syspro Edu database has no dedicated Asset Register module; this query enumerates GL accounts flagged for asset revaluation categories or whose group name indicates assets)
sql: SELECT m.GlCode, m.Description, m.AccountType, m.GlGroup, g.Description AS GroupDescription, m.RevalCategory, c.Description AS RevalCategoryDesc, m.CurrentBalance, m.PrevPerEndBal FROM GenMaster m LEFT JOIN GenGroups g ON g.Company = m.Company AND g.GlGroup = m.GlGroup LEFT JOIN GenCategory c ON c.RevalCategory = m.RevalCategory WHERE m.AccountType = 'A' AND (m.GlGroup LIKE %(asset_group_pattern)s OR m.Description LIKE %(asset_desc_pattern)s OR m.RevalCategory IN (SELECT RevalCategory FROM GenCategory WHERE Description LIKE '%%asset%%' OR Description LIKE '%%equipment%%' OR Description LIKE '%%plant%%')) ORDER BY m.GlGroup, m.GlCode
embedding_text: Fixed-asset GL accounts: this Syspro Edu installation has no Asset Register module (no Asset* tables), so we approximate by selecting Asset (AccountType='A') accounts whose group, description, or revaluation category indicates property/plant/equipment. Tune the LIKE patterns to your chart of accounts.
example_questions: List of fixed assets by category
Show me asset register
Property plant equipment list

description: Asset disposals — credit postings to asset accounts and matching debits to disposal/gain-loss accounts
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.GlCode, m.Description AS AssetAccount, m.GlGroup, t.JnlDate, t.Journal, t.Source, t.Reference, t.Comment, t.EntryValue, t.PostCurrency, t.SubModRef FROM GenTransaction t INNER JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = t.Company AND m.GlCode = t.GlCode WHERE m.AccountType = 'A' AND (m.GlGroup LIKE %(asset_group_pattern)s OR m.Description LIKE %(asset_desc_pattern)s) AND t.JnlDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND t.EntryValue < 0 ORDER BY t.JnlDate DESC, ABS(t.EntryValue) DESC
embedding_text: Approximation of fixed-asset disposals: credit postings (EntryValue < 0) to asset GL accounts. Pair these with the offsetting debits to gain-loss-on-disposal accounts (manually identified) to compute total disposal P&L impact.
example_questions: Assets disposed of this year
Asset sales and write-offs
Fixed asset disposals

description: Asset accounts whose net book value is at or near zero (approximation, since dedicated Asset module is absent)
sql: SELECT m.GlCode, m.Description, m.AccountType, m.GlGroup, g.Description AS GroupDescription, m.CurrentBalance, m.PrevPerEndBal, ABS(m.CurrentBalance) AS AbsBalance FROM GenMaster m LEFT JOIN GenGroups g ON g.Company = m.Company AND g.GlGroup = m.GlGroup WHERE m.AccountType = 'A' AND (m.GlGroup LIKE %(asset_group_pattern)s OR m.Description LIKE %(asset_desc_pattern)s) AND ABS(m.CurrentBalance) <= %(threshold)s ORDER BY m.GlGroup, m.GlCode
embedding_text: Approximation of fully-depreciated assets: returns Asset GL accounts whose current balance is at or below a small threshold. Without an Asset module the query cannot distinguish cost from accumulated depreciation per asset, so it inspects net carrying values at the account level.
example_questions: Fully depreciated assets still in use
Assets with zero balance
Show me fully written-off assets

description: Fixed-asset register from asset GL accounts
sql: SELECT m.GlCode, m.Description, m.GlGroup, g.Description AS GroupName, m.CurrentBalance, m.PrevPerEndBal FROM GenMaster m LEFT JOIN GenGroups g ON g.Company = m.Company AND g.GlGroup = m.GlGroup WHERE m.AccountType = 'A' AND m.GlGroup LIKE %(asset_group_pattern)s ORDER BY m.GlCode
embedding_text: Returns asset GL accounts as a fixed-asset register with current and prior-period-end balances.

description: Contribution margin (revenue less variable cost) by product
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s m.StockCode, m.StockDescription, m.ProductClass, SUM(m.InvoiceQty) AS QtySold, SUM(m.InvoiceValue) AS Revenue, SUM(m.CostValue) AS VariableCost, SUM(m.DiscValue) AS Discount, SUM(m.InvoiceValue) - SUM(m.CostValue) - SUM(m.DiscValue) AS ContributionMargin, CASE WHEN SUM(m.InvoiceValue) <> 0 THEN 100.0 * (SUM(m.InvoiceValue) - SUM(m.CostValue) - SUM(m.DiscValue)) / SUM(m.InvoiceValue) ELSE 0 END AS ContributionMarginPct FROM ArSalesMove m WHERE m.TrnDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND m.StockCode IS NOT NULL AND m.StockCode <> '' GROUP BY m.StockCode, m.StockDescription, m.ProductClass ORDER BY ContributionMargin DESC
embedding_text: Contribution margin per stock code: revenue minus cost minus discount, with margin percentage. Source ArSalesMove. Treats CostValue as the variable cost proxy since the Syspro Edu install does not store separate variable / fixed cost components.
example_questions: Contribution margin analysis
Product CM ranking
Marginal product profit

description: Profit by branch / store — revenue, cost, gross profit from ArSalesMove grouped by branch
sql: SELECT m.Branch, COUNT(DISTINCT m.Invoice) AS Invoices, SUM(m.InvoiceQty) AS QtySold, SUM(m.InvoiceValue) AS Revenue, SUM(m.CostValue) AS Cost, SUM(m.InvoiceValue - m.CostValue) AS GrossProfit, CASE WHEN SUM(m.InvoiceValue) <> 0 THEN 100.0 * SUM(m.InvoiceValue - m.CostValue) / SUM(m.InvoiceValue) ELSE 0 END AS GrossMarginPct FROM ArSalesMove m WHERE m.TrnDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s GROUP BY m.Branch ORDER BY Revenue DESC
embedding_text: Branch-level profit summary from ArSalesMove: revenue (InvoiceValue), cost (CostValue), gross profit, and gross margin percentage over a date range. Used for branch P&L and ranking.
example_questions: Profit by branch / store
Branch performance comparison
Which branch is most profitable?

description: Profit by product line / stock code
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s m.StockCode, m.StockDescription, m.ProductClass, COUNT(DISTINCT m.Invoice) AS Invoices, SUM(m.InvoiceQty) AS QtySold, SUM(m.InvoiceValue) AS Revenue, SUM(m.CostValue) AS Cost, SUM(m.InvoiceValue - m.CostValue) AS GrossProfit, CASE WHEN SUM(m.InvoiceValue) <> 0 THEN 100.0 * SUM(m.InvoiceValue - m.CostValue) / SUM(m.InvoiceValue) ELSE 0 END AS GrossMarginPct FROM ArSalesMove m WHERE m.TrnDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND m.StockCode IS NOT NULL AND m.StockCode <> '' GROUP BY m.StockCode, m.StockDescription, m.ProductClass ORDER BY GrossProfit DESC
embedding_text: Stock-code profitability from ArSalesMove ranked by gross profit. Includes product class so you can roll up. Used for product mix and price-point decisions.
example_questions: Profit by product line
Most profitable products
Stock code margin ranking

description: Gross profit by salesperson
sql: SELECT m.Salesperson, COUNT(DISTINCT m.Invoice) AS Invoices, COUNT(DISTINCT m.Customer) AS UniqueCustomers, SUM(m.InvoiceValue) AS Revenue, SUM(m.CostValue) AS Cost, SUM(m.InvoiceValue - m.CostValue) AS GrossProfit, CASE WHEN SUM(m.InvoiceValue) <> 0 THEN 100.0 * SUM(m.InvoiceValue - m.CostValue) / SUM(m.InvoiceValue) ELSE 0 END AS GrossMarginPct FROM ArSalesMove m WHERE m.TrnDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND m.Salesperson IS NOT NULL AND m.Salesperson <> '' GROUP BY m.Salesperson ORDER BY GrossProfit DESC
embedding_text: Per-salesperson profitability: invoices, unique customers, revenue, cost, gross profit, and margin percentage. Source is ArSalesMove. Used for commission, performance review and contests.
example_questions: Profit by salesperson
Salesperson performance
Sales rep profitability

description: Purchase price variance approximation — compares AP invoice unit values against the average across recent months
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s i.Supplier, s.SupplierName, COUNT(*) AS InvoiceCount, SUM(i.OrigInvValue) AS TotalSpend, AVG(i.OrigInvValue) AS AvgInvoiceValue, MIN(i.OrigInvValue) AS MinInvoiceValue, MAX(i.OrigInvValue) AS MaxInvoiceValue, STDEV(i.OrigInvValue) AS StdDev FROM ApInvoice i LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = i.Supplier WHERE i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s GROUP BY i.Supplier, s.SupplierName HAVING COUNT(*) >= %(min_invoices)s AND STDEV(i.OrigInvValue) > 0 ORDER BY STDEV(i.OrigInvValue) DESC
embedding_text: Purchase price variance proxy by supplier: shows count, min, max, average and standard deviation of invoice values. Since PO line prices are not stored separately, the variance is at invoice-value level. High StdDev highlights suppliers with inconsistent invoicing prices.
example_questions: Purchase price variance last month
PPV by supplier
Where do prices vary the most?

description: Average cost movement for the largest-cost stock items between two periods
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s cur.StockCode, cur.StockDescription, cur.AvgUnitCostCur, prv.AvgUnitCostPrv, cur.AvgUnitCostCur - prv.AvgUnitCostPrv AS Change, CASE WHEN prv.AvgUnitCostPrv <> 0 THEN 100.0 * (cur.AvgUnitCostCur - prv.AvgUnitCostPrv) / prv.AvgUnitCostPrv ELSE NULL END AS ChangePct FROM (SELECT StockCode, MAX(StockDescription) AS StockDescription, CASE WHEN SUM(InvoiceQty) <> 0 THEN SUM(CostValue) / SUM(InvoiceQty) ELSE 0 END AS AvgUnitCostCur FROM ArSalesMove WHERE TrnDate BETWEEN %(cur_start)s AND %(cur_end)s GROUP BY StockCode) cur INNER JOIN (SELECT StockCode, CASE WHEN SUM(InvoiceQty) <> 0 THEN SUM(CostValue) / SUM(InvoiceQty) ELSE 0 END AS AvgUnitCostPrv FROM ArSalesMove WHERE TrnDate BETWEEN %(prv_start)s AND %(prv_end)s GROUP BY StockCode) prv ON prv.StockCode = cur.StockCode WHERE prv.AvgUnitCostPrv <> 0 ORDER BY ABS(cur.AvgUnitCostCur - prv.AvgUnitCostPrv) DESC
embedding_text: Top stock items by average-cost change between two periods. Both averages computed as SUM(CostValue)/SUM(InvoiceQty) from ArSalesMove. Useful for spotting cost spikes in raw materials or traded goods.
example_questions: Average cost changes for raw materials
Which products had biggest cost increase?
Cost movement leaders

description: Material/labour/overhead breakdown by GL group (this Syspro Edu has no BOM/Work-Centre tables; breakdown is derived from GL groups)
sql: SELECT m.GlGroup, g.Description AS GroupDescription, CASE WHEN m.GlGroup LIKE '%%MAT%%' OR m.Description LIKE '%%material%%' OR m.Description LIKE '%%raw%%' THEN 'Material' WHEN m.GlGroup LIKE '%%LAB%%' OR m.Description LIKE '%%labour%%' OR m.Description LIKE '%%labor%%' OR m.Description LIKE '%%wage%%' OR m.Description LIKE '%%salary%%' THEN 'Labour' WHEN m.GlGroup LIKE '%%OVH%%' OR m.Description LIKE '%%overhead%%' OR m.Description LIKE '%%factory%%' THEN 'Overhead' ELSE 'Other' END AS CostCategory, COUNT(*) AS AccountCount, SUM(ISNULL((CASE %(period)s WHEN 1 THEN h.ClosingBalPer1 - h.BeginYearBalance WHEN 2 THEN h.ClosingBalPer2 - h.ClosingBalPer1 WHEN 3 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 - h.ClosingBalPer2 WHEN 4 THEN h.ClosingBalPer4 - h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 5 THEN h.ClosingBalPer5 - h.ClosingBalPer4 WHEN 6 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 - h.ClosingBalPer5 WHEN 7 THEN h.ClosingBalPer7 - h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 8 THEN h.ClosingBalPer8 - h.ClosingBalPer7 WHEN 9 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 - h.ClosingBalPer8 WHEN 10 THEN h.ClosingBalPer10 - h.ClosingBalPer9 WHEN 11 THEN h.ClosingBalPer11 - h.ClosingBalPer10 WHEN 12 THEN h.ClosingBalPer12 - h.ClosingBalPer11 END), 0)) AS PeriodMovement FROM GenMaster m LEFT JOIN GenGroups g ON g.Company = m.Company AND g.GlGroup = m.GlGroup LEFT JOIN GenHistory h ON h.Company = m.Company AND h.GlCode = m.GlCode AND h.GlYear = %(gl_year)s WHERE m.AccountType = 'E' GROUP BY m.GlGroup, g.Description, CASE WHEN m.GlGroup LIKE '%%MAT%%' OR m.Description LIKE '%%material%%' OR m.Description LIKE '%%raw%%' THEN 'Material' WHEN m.GlGroup LIKE '%%LAB%%' OR m.Description LIKE '%%labour%%' OR m.Description LIKE '%%labor%%' OR m.Description LIKE '%%wage%%' OR m.Description LIKE '%%salary%%' THEN 'Labour' WHEN m.GlGroup LIKE '%%OVH%%' OR m.Description LIKE '%%overhead%%' OR m.Description LIKE '%%factory%%' THEN 'Overhead' ELSE 'Other' END ORDER BY CostCategory, m.GlGroup
embedding_text: Material/Labour/Overhead classification of expense GL accounts based on GlGroup pattern and account description, with period movement from GenHistory. Adjust the LIKE patterns to your chart-of-accounts conventions.
example_questions: Material labour overhead breakdown
MLO costs for period
Manufacturing cost categories

description: Cost roll-up history approximation — monthly weighted average cost per stock code
sql: SELECT m.StockCode, m.StockDescription, m.TrnYear, m.TrnMonth, SUM(m.InvoiceQty) AS TotalQty, SUM(m.CostValue) AS TotalCost, CASE WHEN SUM(m.InvoiceQty) <> 0 THEN SUM(m.CostValue) / SUM(m.InvoiceQty) ELSE 0 END AS WeightedAvgUnitCost FROM ArSalesMove m WHERE m.TrnDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND (%(stock_code)s IS NULL OR m.StockCode = %(stock_code)s) AND m.StockCode IS NOT NULL AND m.StockCode <> '' GROUP BY m.StockCode, m.StockDescription, m.TrnYear, m.TrnMonth ORDER BY m.StockCode, m.TrnYear, m.TrnMonth
embedding_text: Monthly weighted-average unit cost from ArSalesMove per stock code — a substitute for a dedicated cost roll-up history table. Pass an optional stock code to drill into one item.
example_questions: Cost roll-up history for last month
Monthly cost history per product
How has unit cost trended?

description: Standard vs actual cost approximation (no dedicated costing module — uses ArSalesMove cost values vs original sales)
sql: SELECT m.StockCode, m.StockDescription, COUNT(*) AS SaleCount, SUM(m.InvoiceQty) AS TotalQty, SUM(m.InvoiceValue) AS TotalRevenue, SUM(m.CostValue) AS TotalActualCost, SUM(m.InvoiceValue - m.CostValue) AS TotalGrossProfit, CASE WHEN SUM(m.InvoiceQty) <> 0 THEN SUM(m.CostValue) / SUM(m.InvoiceQty) ELSE 0 END AS AvgUnitActualCost, CASE WHEN SUM(m.InvoiceValue) <> 0 THEN 100.0 * SUM(m.InvoiceValue - m.CostValue) / SUM(m.InvoiceValue) ELSE 0 END AS GrossMarginPct FROM ArSalesMove m WHERE m.TrnDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND m.StockCode IS NOT NULL AND m.StockCode <> '' GROUP BY m.StockCode, m.StockDescription ORDER BY TotalRevenue DESC
embedding_text: Product cost analysis from ArSalesMove: actual cost (CostValue) and revenue (InvoiceValue) per stock code over a date range. Standard cost is not stored separately in this Syspro Edu installation; compare AvgUnitActualCost across periods to detect cost drift.
example_questions: Standard cost vs actual cost
Product cost analysis
Unit cost trends

description: Cost variance by product comparing current period unit cost to prior period unit cost
sql: SELECT cur.StockCode, cur.StockDescription, cur.QtyCur, cur.CostCur, cur.UnitCostCur, prv.QtyPrv, prv.CostPrv, prv.UnitCostPrv, cur.UnitCostCur - prv.UnitCostPrv AS UnitCostVariance, CASE WHEN prv.UnitCostPrv <> 0 THEN 100.0 * (cur.UnitCostCur - prv.UnitCostPrv) / prv.UnitCostPrv ELSE NULL END AS UnitCostVariancePct FROM (SELECT StockCode, MAX(StockDescription) AS StockDescription, SUM(InvoiceQty) AS QtyCur, SUM(CostValue) AS CostCur, CASE WHEN SUM(InvoiceQty) <> 0 THEN SUM(CostValue) / SUM(InvoiceQty) ELSE 0 END AS UnitCostCur FROM ArSalesMove WHERE TrnDate BETWEEN %(cur_start)s AND %(cur_end)s AND StockCode IS NOT NULL AND StockCode <> '' GROUP BY StockCode) cur INNER JOIN (SELECT StockCode, SUM(InvoiceQty) AS QtyPrv, SUM(CostValue) AS CostPrv, CASE WHEN SUM(InvoiceQty) <> 0 THEN SUM(CostValue) / SUM(InvoiceQty) ELSE 0 END AS UnitCostPrv FROM ArSalesMove WHERE TrnDate BETWEEN %(prv_start)s AND %(prv_end)s AND StockCode IS NOT NULL AND StockCode <> '' GROUP BY StockCode) prv ON prv.StockCode = cur.StockCode ORDER BY ABS(cur.UnitCostCur - prv.UnitCostPrv) DESC
embedding_text: Compares current-period unit cost (CostValue / InvoiceQty from ArSalesMove) against prior-period unit cost for the same stock code, producing variance and percentage. Useful as a proxy for material, labour, overhead variance where dedicated costing data is absent.
example_questions: Material, labour, overhead variances
Cost variance by product
Where did costs change?

description: Labour-account GL activity grouped by job reference (no Work-In-Progress module in this install)
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.SubModJob AS Job, t.SubModEmployee AS Employee, t.SubModWorkCenter AS WorkCentre, t.GlCode, m.Description, t.JnlDate, t.Journal, SUM(t.EntryValue) AS LabourValue, COUNT(*) AS Postings FROM GenTransaction t INNER JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = t.Company AND m.GlCode = t.GlCode WHERE t.JnlDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND t.SubModJob IS NOT NULL AND t.SubModJob <> '' AND (m.Description LIKE '%%labour%%' OR m.Description LIKE '%%labor%%' OR m.Description LIKE '%%wage%%' OR m.GlGroup LIKE '%%LAB%%') GROUP BY t.SubModJob, t.SubModEmployee, t.SubModWorkCenter, t.GlCode, m.Description, t.JnlDate, t.Journal ORDER BY t.SubModJob, t.JnlDate
embedding_text: Labour value posted by job using GenTransaction.SubModJob, SubModEmployee, SubModWorkCenter cross-references. Filters to GL accounts whose description suggests labour/wages. Hour quantities are not stored in this install, so only monetary value is available.
example_questions: Labour hours by project
Job labour costs
Project labour spend

description: Material consumption by job from GL postings tagged with SubModJob
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.SubModJob AS Job, t.SubModStock AS StockCode, t.GlCode, m.Description, SUM(t.EntryValue) AS MaterialValue, COUNT(*) AS Postings FROM GenTransaction t INNER JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = t.Company AND m.GlCode = t.GlCode WHERE t.JnlDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND t.SubModJob IS NOT NULL AND t.SubModJob <> '' AND (m.Description LIKE '%%material%%' OR m.Description LIKE '%%raw%%' OR m.GlGroup LIKE '%%MAT%%') GROUP BY t.SubModJob, t.SubModStock, t.GlCode, m.Description ORDER BY t.SubModJob, MaterialValue DESC
embedding_text: Material spend by job: groups GenTransaction lines that carry a SubModJob and stock-code reference, filtered to material expense GL accounts. Aggregates value and posting count.

description: Overhead allocation to jobs / GL accounts via apportionment rules
sql: SELECT a.GlCode AS SourceAccount, m.Description AS SourceDescription, ap.PostFrequency, ap.Authorised, ap.Reference, ap.Notation FROM GenApportion a INNER JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = a.Company AND m.GlCode = a.GlCode LEFT JOIN GenRecJnlCtl ap ON ap.JournalCode = a.GlCode WHERE (m.GlGroup LIKE '%%OVH%%' OR m.Description LIKE '%%overhead%%' OR m.Description LIKE '%%apportion%%') ORDER BY a.GlCode
embedding_text: Overhead apportionment setup from GenApportion joined to GenMaster. Where the apportionment is tied to a recurring journal, also pulls GenRecJnlCtl details. Used to review overhead allocation rules.
example_questions: Overhead allocation to projects
Apportionment rules
Overhead absorption setup

description: Project / job actual vs budget — uses GenCommitment for budget and GenCommitDetail for actual postings
sql: SELECT cm.Company, cm.GlCode, m.Description AS AccountName, cm.CommitmentType, cm.PrevCommitment + cm.Commitment1 + cm.Commitment2 + cm.Commitment3 + cm.Commitment4 + cm.Commitment5 + cm.Commitment6 + cm.Commitment7 + cm.Commitment8 + cm.Commitment9 + cm.Commitment10 + cm.Commitment11 + cm.Commitment12 + cm.Commitment13 + cm.FutureCommitment AS TotalCommitted, cm.PrevUncommitted + cm.Uncommitted1 + cm.Uncommitted2 + cm.Uncommitted3 + cm.Uncommitted4 + cm.Uncommitted5 + cm.Uncommitted6 + cm.Uncommitted7 + cm.Uncommitted8 + cm.Uncommitted9 + cm.Uncommitted10 + cm.Uncommitted11 + cm.Uncommitted12 + cm.Uncommitted13 + cm.FutureUncommitted AS TotalUncommitted, (cm.PrevCommitment + cm.Commitment1 + cm.Commitment2 + cm.Commitment3 + cm.Commitment4 + cm.Commitment5 + cm.Commitment6 + cm.Commitment7 + cm.Commitment8 + cm.Commitment9 + cm.Commitment10 + cm.Commitment11 + cm.Commitment12 + cm.Commitment13 + cm.FutureCommitment) - (cm.PrevUncommitted + cm.Uncommitted1 + cm.Uncommitted2 + cm.Uncommitted3 + cm.Uncommitted4 + cm.Uncommitted5 + cm.Uncommitted6 + cm.Uncommitted7 + cm.Uncommitted8 + cm.Uncommitted9 + cm.Uncommitted10 + cm.Uncommitted11 + cm.Uncommitted12 + cm.Uncommitted13 + cm.FutureUncommitted) AS Variance FROM GenCommitment cm LEFT JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = cm.Company AND m.GlCode = cm.GlCode WHERE (%(gl_code)s IS NULL OR cm.GlCode = %(gl_code)s) ORDER BY cm.GlCode
embedding_text: Project/job actual-vs-committed using GenCommitment: sums all period commitments (Commitment1-13 plus prev and future) vs all uncommitted amounts. Use this in lieu of a Project module since the Syspro Edu install lacks dedicated Prj* tables.
example_questions: Project actual vs budget for project XYZ
Commitment vs spend report
Project cost tracking

description: Sales, cost and margin by stock code
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s m.StockCode, m.StockDescription, SUM(m.InvoiceValue) AS Sales, SUM(m.CostValue) AS Cost, SUM(m.InvoiceValue) - SUM(m.CostValue) AS Margin FROM ArSalesMove m WHERE m.TrnDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s GROUP BY m.StockCode, m.StockDescription ORDER BY Margin DESC
embedding_text: Computes sales, cost of sales and gross margin per stock code for product-costing analysis.

description: Intercompany receivable/payable balances per company pair
sql: SELECT t.Company AS PrimaryCompany, t.SecondaryCompany, m.AccountType, CASE m.AccountType WHEN 'A' THEN 'Receivable' WHEN 'L' THEN 'Payable' ELSE 'Other' END AS BalanceType, COUNT(*) AS EntryCount, SUM(t.EntryValue) AS NetBalance FROM GenTransaction t INNER JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = t.Company AND m.GlCode = t.GlCode WHERE (t.InterCompanyTrx = 'Y' OR t.GroupInterCompany = 'Y') AND t.SecondaryCompany IS NOT NULL AND t.SecondaryCompany <> '' AND t.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND t.GlPeriod <= %(period)s GROUP BY t.Company, t.SecondaryCompany, m.AccountType ORDER BY t.Company, t.SecondaryCompany
embedding_text: Net intercompany balance per Primary-Secondary company pair, classified as Receivable (Asset accounts) or Payable (Liability accounts). Aggregates GenTransaction joined to GenMaster up to the requested period.
example_questions: Intercompany balances – payable/receivable
IC balances by company pair
Related party balances

description: All intercompany transactions across AP, AR and GL in a date range
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s 'GL' AS Source, t.Company AS PrimaryCompany, t.SecondaryCompany, t.GlCode, t.JnlDate, t.Journal, t.Reference, t.EntryValue, t.PostCurrency, t.SubModSupplier, t.SubModCustomer FROM GenTransaction t WHERE t.JnlDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND (t.InterCompanyTrx = 'Y' OR t.GroupInterCompany = 'Y') UNION ALL SELECT 'AP' AS Source, p.PrimaryCompany, p.SecondaryCompany, NULL AS GlCode, p.PaymDate AS JnlDate, NULL AS Journal, p.Invoice AS Reference, p.WithTaxValue AS EntryValue, NULL AS PostCurrency, p.Supplier AS SubModSupplier, NULL AS SubModCustomer FROM ApWithholdTax p WHERE p.PaymDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND p.GroupInterCompany = 'Y' ORDER BY 5 DESC
embedding_text: Union of intercompany entries from GenTransaction (InterCompanyTrx or GroupInterCompany flag) and ApWithholdTax (GroupInterCompany flag) for cross-company transactions. Identifies primary vs secondary company for consolidation.
example_questions: Intercompany transactions list
Show interco entries for the period
Cross-company transactions

description: Transfer pricing review — intercompany sales values with margins compared to standard customer sales
sql: SELECT s.Customer, c.Name AS CustomerName, c.Nationality AS CustomerCountry, c.MasterAccount, s.StockCode, COUNT(*) AS LineCount, SUM(s.InvoiceQty) AS TotalQty, SUM(s.InvoiceValue) AS NetSalesValue, SUM(s.CostValue) AS CostValue, SUM(s.InvoiceValue - s.CostValue) AS GrossProfit, CASE WHEN SUM(s.InvoiceValue) <> 0 THEN 100.0 * SUM(s.InvoiceValue - s.CostValue) / SUM(s.InvoiceValue) ELSE NULL END AS MarginPct FROM ArSalesMove s INNER JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = s.Customer WHERE s.TrnDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND (c.IbtCustomer = 'Y' OR c.MasterAccount IS NOT NULL AND c.MasterAccount <> '') GROUP BY s.Customer, c.Name, c.Nationality, c.MasterAccount, s.StockCode ORDER BY s.Customer, s.StockCode
embedding_text: Aggregates ArSalesMove by intercompany customer (IbtCustomer flag or non-null MasterAccount) and stock code. Computes gross profit and margin percentage per product to identify transfer pricing adjustments needed.
example_questions: Transfer pricing adjustments
Intercompany sales margins
TP review by product

description: Intercompany entries where the matching counter-entry cannot be found
sql: SELECT t.Company AS PrimaryCompany, t.SecondaryCompany, t.JnlDate, t.Journal, t.Reference, t.EntryValue, t.PostCurrency, t.SubModRef, t.OriginZoomKey FROM GenTransaction t WHERE t.JnlDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND (t.InterCompanyTrx = 'Y' OR t.GroupInterCompany = 'Y') AND t.SecondaryCompany IS NOT NULL AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM GenTransaction t2 WHERE t2.Company = t.SecondaryCompany AND t2.SecondaryCompany = t.Company AND t2.JnlDate = t.JnlDate AND ABS(ABS(t2.EntryValue) - ABS(t.EntryValue)) < 0.01) ORDER BY t.JnlDate DESC, ABS(t.EntryValue) DESC
embedding_text: Intercompany GenTransaction lines for which no matching counter-entry exists in the opposite direction in another company on the same date and amount. Reveals interco-elimination breaks that need fixing before consolidation.
example_questions: Unmatched intercompany transactions
IC entries with no counterparty match
Intercompany reconciliation gaps

embedding_text: Aggregates total credit grouped by period month to date. Built on GenTransaction; useful for intercompany analysis and management reporting.

description: Full audit trail of financial posts in a date range
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.Company, t.GlYear, t.GlPeriod, t.GlCode, m.Description AS GlAccount, t.JnlDate, t.Journal, t.Source, t.Origin, t.Reference, t.Comment, t.EntryValue, t.PostCurrency, c.Operator AS PostedBy, c.AuthorisedBy, c.PostDate FROM GenTransaction t LEFT JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = t.Company AND m.GlCode = t.GlCode LEFT JOIN GenJournalCtl c ON c.GlYear = t.GlYear AND c.GlPeriod = t.GlPeriod AND c.GlJournal = t.Journal WHERE t.JnlDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY t.JnlDate DESC, t.Journal DESC
embedding_text: Combined audit trail joining GenTransaction with GenJournalCtl to show every GL line along with who posted, who authorised, and the post date. Used by internal/external auditors for sampling.
example_questions: Audit trail for all financial posts last week
Show me complete posting audit log
Who posted GL entries last month

description: Segregation of duties violations — same user posting and authorising journals
sql: SELECT c.GlYear, c.GlPeriod, c.GlJournal, c.JournalDate, c.PostDate, c.Source, c.Operator AS PostedBy, c.AuthorisedBy, c.PostedBy AS PostedByConfirm, c.Reference, c.DebitAmount, c.CreditAmount FROM GenJournalCtl c WHERE c.JournalDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND c.Authorised = 'Y' AND c.AuthorisedBy IS NOT NULL AND c.AuthorisedBy <> '' AND (c.AuthorisedBy = c.Operator OR c.AuthorisedBy = c.PostedBy) ORDER BY c.JournalDate DESC
embedding_text: Identifies GenJournalCtl rows where the AuthorisedBy operator equals the entry Operator or PostedBy — a segregation-of-duties violation. Used for internal control audits.
example_questions: Segregation of duties violations
Same user posted and approved
SoD control breaks

description: Journal entries that lack approval/authorisation
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s c.GlYear, c.GlPeriod, c.GlJournal, c.JournalDate, c.Source, c.Operator, c.Reference, c.Notation, c.DebitAmount, c.CreditAmount, c.NumOfEntries, c.JnlStatus, c.Authorised, c.PostDate FROM GenJournalCtl c WHERE c.JournalDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND (c.Authorised <> 'Y' OR c.Authorised IS NULL) ORDER BY c.JournalDate DESC, ABS(c.DebitAmount) DESC
embedding_text: GenJournalCtl entries that have not been authorised (Authorised <> 'Y'). May or may not be posted. Used for approval-workflow audit.
example_questions: Unapproved journal entries
Pending approval journals
Journals lacking authorisation

description: User activity log on financial modules (event log)
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s e.EventDate, e.EventTime, e.Operator, e.EventModule, e.EventCode, e.EventDesc, e.EventName, e.EventType, e.Description, e.Key1, e.Key2, e.ProgGenEvent FROM AdmEventLog e WHERE e.EventDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND (%(module)s IS NULL OR e.EventModule = %(module)s) ORDER BY e.EventDate DESC, e.EventTime DESC
embedding_text: User activity events from AdmEventLog including operator, module, event description and originating program. Optionally filter by module letter (A=AP, R=AR, G=GL, C=Cash). Used for SOX/IT access auditing.
example_questions: User login / access log for finance module
User activity on AP module
Operator audit trail

description: All unposted journals across modules
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s jc.GlYear, jc.GlPeriod, jc.GlJournal, jc.JournalDate, jc.Source, jc.DebitAmount, jc.CreditAmount, jc.JnlStatus, jc.Operator FROM GenJournalCtl jc WHERE jc.JnlStatus <> 'P' ORDER BY jc.GlYear DESC, jc.GlPeriod DESC
embedding_text: Lists journals not yet posted across all modules for closing and audit review.
example_questions: Unposted journals • Pending postings audit • Journals not yet posted

description: Journals where debits do not equal credits
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s jc.GlJournal, jc.JournalDate, jc.Source, jc.DebitAmount, jc.CreditAmount, jc.DebitAmount - jc.CreditAmount AS Difference, jc.Operator FROM GenJournalCtl jc WHERE ABS(jc.DebitAmount - jc.CreditAmount) > 0.01 ORDER BY ABS(jc.DebitAmount - jc.CreditAmount) DESC
embedding_text: Detects journals whose debit and credit totals differ, a fundamental integrity check.
example_questions: Unbalanced journals • Journals where debits not equal credits • Integrity check on postings

embedding_text: Aggregates largest debit grouped by journal status month to date. Built on GenJournalCtl; useful for compliance audit analysis and management reporting.
example_questions: Largest Debit by journal status month to date • Show largest debit per journal status month to date • Largest Debit breakdown by journal status month to date

description: Combined open/closed period status across GL, AP and Cash modules
sql: SELECT 'GL' AS Module, g.GlYear AS CurrentYear, g.GlPeriod AS CurrentPeriod, g.CurYrPrdStat1 AS Per1, g.CurYrPrdStat2 AS Per2, g.CurYrPrdStat3 AS Per3, g.CurYrPrdStat4 AS Per4, g.CurYrPrdStat5 AS Per5, g.CurYrPrdStat6 AS Per6, g.CurYrPrdStat7 AS Per7, g.CurYrPrdStat8 AS Per8, g.CurYrPrdStat9 AS Per9, g.CurYrPrdStat10 AS Per10, g.CurYrPrdStat11 AS Per11, g.CurYrPrdStat12 AS Per12 FROM GenControl g UNION ALL SELECT 'AP' AS Module, a.FinYear, a.FinPeriod, a.CurYrMthStat1, a.CurYrMthStat2, a.CurYrMthStat3, a.CurYrMthStat4, a.CurYrMthStat5, a.CurYrMthStat6, a.CurYrMthStat7, a.CurYrMthStat8, a.CurYrMthStat9, a.CurYrMthStat10, a.CurYrMthStat11, a.CurYrMthStat12 FROM ApControl a UNION ALL SELECT 'AR' AS Module, ar.FinYear, ar.FinPeriod, ar.CurYrMthStat1, ar.CurYrMthStat2, ar.CurYrMthStat3, ar.CurYrMthStat4, ar.CurYrMthStat5, ar.CurYrMthStat6, ar.CurYrMthStat7, ar.CurYrMthStat8, ar.CurYrMthStat9, ar.CurYrMthStat10, ar.CurYrMthStat11, ar.CurYrMthStat12 FROM ArControl ar UNION ALL SELECT 'Cash' AS Module, c.CurrentFinYear, c.CurrentFinMonth, c.CurYrMthStat1, c.CurYrMthStat2, c.CurYrMthStat3, c.CurYrMthStat4, c.CurYrMthStat5, c.CurYrMthStat6, c.CurYrMthStat7, c.CurYrMthStat8, c.CurYrMthStat9, c.CurYrMthStat10, c.CurYrMthStat11, c.CurYrMthStat12 FROM CshControl c
embedding_text: Union of period status flags across GenControl, ApControl, ArControl, and CshControl for all 12 periods. One row per module showing the current period and open/closed flags (O = Open, C = Closed) for each period.
example_questions: Open periods with closing status
Module wise period status
GL AP AR Cash open periods

description: Recurring entries due in the next period across GL and other modules
sql: SELECT 'GL' AS Module, c.JournalCode AS Code, c.Reference, c.Notation, c.NextPostYear AS Year, c.NextPostPeriod AS Period, c.PostFrequency, c.NumOfEntries AS EntryCount, c.DebitAmount, c.CreditAmount, c.Authorised, c.AuthorisedBy FROM GenRecJnlCtl c WHERE c.NextPostYear = %(gl_year)s AND c.NextPostPeriod = %(period)s ORDER BY c.JournalCode
embedding_text: Recurring journals from GenRecJnlCtl scheduled to post into the specified year and period. Used during month-end planning to ensure all recurring accruals/allocations are run.
example_questions: Recurring entries due for next period
Scheduled accruals for next month
Recurring journals to be posted

description: All unposted batches across modules for a period
sql: SELECT 'GL' AS Module, c.GlYear AS Year, c.GlPeriod AS Period, c.GlJournal AS Journal, c.JournalDate, c.Source, c.Operator, c.Reference, c.DebitAmount, c.CreditAmount FROM GenJournalCtl c WHERE c.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND c.GlPeriod = %(period)s AND (c.JnlStatus IN ('U','P') OR c.PostDate IS NULL) UNION ALL SELECT 'AP' AS Module, a.FinYear, a.FinMonth, a.Journal, a.JournalDate, NULL AS Source, a.Operator, NULL AS Reference, a.InvoiceValue, a.CreditMemoValue FROM ApJnlControl a WHERE a.FinYear = %(gl_year)s AND a.FinMonth = %(period)s AND a.JournalStatus <> 'P' UNION ALL SELECT 'Cash' AS Module, ch.GlYear, ch.GlPeriod, ch.Journal, ch.JnlDate, ch.Source, ch.Operator, NULL AS Reference, NULL AS DebitAmount, NULL AS CreditAmount FROM CshJnlCtl ch WHERE ch.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND ch.GlPeriod = %(period)s AND ISNULL(ch.GlIntUpdated, 'N') <> 'Y' ORDER BY 1, 4
embedding_text: Unposted batches across GL (GenJournalCtl), AP (ApJnlControl), and Cash (CshJnlCtl) for a given period and year. Must be cleared before period closing.
example_questions: Unposted batches by period
Pending postings in all modules
Closing prerequisites unposted entries

description: Year-end carry-forward — opening balances roll-forward for next year
sql: SELECT m.Company, m.GlCode, m.Description, m.AccountType, m.GlGroup, m.RetEarnsAccount, ISNULL(h_prev.ClosingBalPer12, 0) AS PriorYearClosingBalance, ISNULL(h_curr.BeginYearBalance, 0) AS CurrentYearOpeningBalance, ISNULL(h_curr.BeginYearBalance, 0) - ISNULL(h_prev.ClosingBalPer12, 0) AS RolloverDifference FROM GenMaster m LEFT JOIN GenHistory h_prev ON h_prev.Company = m.Company AND h_prev.GlCode = m.GlCode AND h_prev.GlYear = %(gl_year)s - 1 LEFT JOIN GenHistory h_curr ON h_curr.Company = m.Company AND h_curr.GlCode = m.GlCode AND h_curr.GlYear = %(gl_year)s WHERE m.AccountType IN ('A','L','C') ORDER BY ABS(ISNULL(h_curr.BeginYearBalance, 0) - ISNULL(h_prev.ClosingBalPer12, 0)) DESC
embedding_text: Validates year-end rollover by comparing prior year period 12 closing balance to current year opening balance per balance-sheet GL account (A, L, C). Any non-zero RolloverDifference indicates a rollover error.
example_questions: Year-end carry forward details
Validate opening balances
Rollover from prior year

description: Unposted journals by source for closing checklist
sql: SELECT jc.Source, COUNT(*) AS UnpostedJournals, SUM(jc.DebitAmount) AS Debit, SUM(jc.CreditAmount) AS Credit FROM GenJournalCtl jc WHERE jc.JnlStatus <> 'P' AND jc.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND jc.GlPeriod = %(period)s GROUP BY jc.Source ORDER BY UnpostedJournals DESC
embedding_text: Counts unposted journals by source for a given period as a period-end closing checklist.
example_questions: Unposted journals by source for closing • Closing checklist by module • What is left to post this period?

description: Posted Journals by source for a date range
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s jc.Source, SUM(CASE WHEN jc.JnlStatus = 'P' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS PostedJournals FROM GenJournalCtl jc WHERE jc.JournalDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s GROUP BY jc.Source ORDER BY PostedJournals DESC
embedding_text: Aggregates posted journals grouped by source for a date range. Built on GenJournalCtl; useful for period closing analysis and management reporting.

description: Net currency exposure summary across all modules (AP + AR + bank)
sql: SELECT Currency, SUM(ApBalance) AS ApBalance, SUM(ArBalance) AS ArBalance, SUM(BankBalance) AS BankBalance, SUM(ArBalance) - SUM(ApBalance) + SUM(BankBalance) AS NetExposure FROM (SELECT i.Currency, SUM(i.MthInvBal1) AS ApBalance, CAST(0 AS decimal(18,2)) AS ArBalance, CAST(0 AS decimal(18,2)) AS BankBalance FROM ApInvoice i WHERE i.MthInvBal1 <> 0 AND i.Currency IS NOT NULL AND i.Currency <> '' AND i.Currency <> %(base_currency)s GROUP BY i.Currency UNION ALL SELECT i.PostCurrency AS Currency, CAST(0 AS decimal(18,2)) AS ApBalance, SUM(i.InvoiceBal1) AS ArBalance, CAST(0 AS decimal(18,2)) AS BankBalance FROM ArInvoice i WHERE i.InvoiceBal1 <> 0 AND i.PostCurrency IS NOT NULL AND i.PostCurrency <> '' AND i.PostCurrency <> %(base_currency)s GROUP BY i.PostCurrency UNION ALL SELECT b.BankCurrency AS Currency, CAST(0 AS decimal(18,2)) AS ApBalance, CAST(0 AS decimal(18,2)) AS ArBalance, SUM(b.CurrencyValue) AS BankBalance FROM CshTransactions b WHERE b.BankCurrency IS NOT NULL AND b.BankCurrency <> '' AND b.BankCurrency <> %(base_currency)s GROUP BY b.BankCurrency) e GROUP BY Currency ORDER BY ABS(SUM(ArBalance) - SUM(ApBalance) + SUM(BankBalance)) DESC
embedding_text: Per-currency net exposure: aggregates open AP balances (negative obligation), AR balances (asset), and bank cash balances by currency from ApInvoice, ArInvoice, and CshTransactions. Filters out base currency.
example_questions: Currency exposure report
FX net position by currency
Forex risk summary

description: Exchange rate history for a currency with change reasons
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s h.Currency, h.DateRateChange, h.TimeRateChange, h.ConvRate, h.MulDiv, h.Triangulated, h.TriangCurrency, h.Operator, h.ReasonForChange FROM GenHistCur h WHERE h.Currency = %(currency)s AND (%(start_date)s IS NULL OR h.DateRateChange >= %(start_date)s) AND (%(end_date)s IS NULL OR h.DateRateChange <= %(end_date)s) ORDER BY h.DateRateChange DESC, h.TimeRateChange DESC
embedding_text: Full conversion rate history for a single currency from GenHistCur. Includes who changed the rate (Operator), when (DateRateChange/TimeRateChange) and the stated reason. Useful for FX audit.
example_questions: Exchange rate history for EUR
Show FX rate timeline for USD
When did the rate change?

description: Foreign currency AR and AP balances revalued at month-end rate
sql: SELECT 'AP' AS Module, i.Supplier AS PartyCode, s.SupplierName AS PartyName, i.Invoice, i.InvoiceDate, i.DueDate, i.Currency, i.OrigInvValue AS OriginalValue, i.CurrencyValue AS ForeignBalance, i.MthInvBal1 AS LocalBalance, l.LastRate, l.MthEndExchangeRate FROM ApInvoice i INNER JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = i.Supplier LEFT JOIN GenRevalLog l ON l.PostCurrency = i.Currency AND l.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND l.GlPeriod = %(period)s WHERE i.MthInvBal1 <> 0 AND i.Currency IS NOT NULL AND i.Currency <> '' AND i.Currency <> %(base_currency)s UNION ALL SELECT 'AR' AS Module, i.Customer AS PartyCode, c.Name AS PartyName, i.Invoice, i.InvoiceDate, NULL AS DueDate, i.PostCurrency AS Currency, i.CurrencyValue AS OriginalValue, i.CurrencyValue AS ForeignBalance, i.InvoiceBal1 AS LocalBalance, l.LastRate, l.MthEndExchangeRate FROM ArInvoice i INNER JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = i.Customer LEFT JOIN GenRevalLog l ON l.PostCurrency = i.PostCurrency AND l.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND l.GlPeriod = %(period)s WHERE i.InvoiceBal1 <> 0 AND i.PostCurrency IS NOT NULL AND i.PostCurrency <> '' AND i.PostCurrency <> %(base_currency)s ORDER BY 1, 12 DESC
embedding_text: Open foreign-currency AP and AR invoice balances joined to GenRevalLog month-end rate. Used to compute revalued local-currency exposure at period end.
example_questions: Revalued AR/AP balances as of month end
Foreign currency open balances
FX exposure by invoice

description: Unrealized forex gain/loss from invoice revaluations (AP + AR)
sql: SELECT 'AP' AS Module, r.Supplier AS PartyCode, s.SupplierName AS PartyName, r.Invoice, r.JournalDate AS RevalDate, r.TrnYear, r.TrnMonth, r.PostCurrency, r.BalRevalued, r.RevalValue, r.BefValue, r.RevalValue - r.BefValue AS GainLoss FROM ApInvoiceReval r LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = r.Supplier WHERE r.JournalDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s UNION ALL SELECT 'AR' AS Module, r.Customer AS PartyCode, c.Name AS PartyName, r.Invoice, r.JournalDate AS RevalDate, r.TrnYear, r.TrnMonth, r.PostCurrency, r.BalRevalued, r.RevalValue, r.BefValue, r.RevalValue - r.BefValue AS GainLoss FROM ArInvoiceReval r LEFT JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = r.Customer WHERE r.JournalDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY ABS(11) DESC
embedding_text: Union of AP (ApInvoiceReval) and AR (ArInvoiceReval) revaluation entries — invoice value before vs after revaluation gives unrealized gain/loss. Combined view for forex P&L analysis.
example_questions: Unrealized forex gain/loss by account
FX revaluation gain loss
Open exposure gain/loss

embedding_text: Aggregates ap invoice count grouped by month and currency over the last 3 months. Built on ApInvoice; useful for foreign currency analysis and management reporting.

description: Drill from an AP payment to its bank/cheque entry and the GL postings
sql: SELECT p.Supplier, s.SupplierName, p.Invoice, p.PaymentNumber, p.Cheque, p.ChequeRegister, p.ChequeDate, p.GrossValue, p.NetValue, p.WithTaxValue, p.Currency, p.Bank, ct.TrnDate AS BankTrnDate, ct.TrnValue AS BankTrnValue, ct.Narration AS BankNarration, ct.ReconciledFlag, t.GlCode, m.Description AS GlAccount, t.JnlDate AS GlJnlDate, t.Journal AS GlJournal, t.EntryValue AS GlEntryValue, t.Source AS GlSource FROM CshApPayments p LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = p.Supplier LEFT JOIN CshTransactions ct ON ct.Bank = p.Bank AND ct.Cheque = p.Cheque AND ct.Supplier = p.Supplier LEFT JOIN GenTransaction t ON t.SubModCheck = CAST(p.Cheque AS varchar) AND t.SubModBank = p.Bank AND t.SubModSupplier = p.Supplier LEFT JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = t.Company AND m.GlCode = t.GlCode WHERE p.Supplier = %(supplier)s AND (%(payment_number)s IS NULL OR p.PaymentNumber = %(payment_number)s) AND (%(cheque)s IS NULL OR p.Cheque = %(cheque)s) ORDER BY p.CbTrnDate, t.JnlDate, t.Journal
embedding_text: Full drill from an AP payment (CshApPayments) to the bank transaction (CshTransactions) and onward to the GL postings (GenTransaction matched by SubModCheck/SubModBank/SubModSupplier). Suppliers names from ApSupplier, GL names from GenMaster.
example_questions: Show GL entries for AP payment PMT001
Drill from supplier cheque to GL
AP payment to bank to GL trace

description: Drill from an AR invoice to the GL journal entries it created
sql: SELECT i.Customer, c.Name AS CustomerName, i.Invoice, i.DocumentType, i.InvoiceDate, i.CurrencyValue AS InvoiceValue, i.PostCurrency, t.Company, t.GlCode, m.Description AS GlAccount, t.JnlDate, t.Journal, t.Source, t.Reference, t.EntryValue, t.PostCurrency AS GlCurrency FROM ArInvoice i LEFT JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = i.Customer LEFT JOIN GenTransaction t ON t.SubModArInvoice = i.Invoice AND t.SubModCustomer = i.Customer LEFT JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = t.Company AND m.GlCode = t.GlCode WHERE i.Customer = %(customer)s AND i.Invoice = %(invoice)s ORDER BY t.JnlDate, t.Journal
embedding_text: Drill from a specific AR invoice to all GL entries that reference it via GenTransaction.SubModArInvoice and SubModCustomer. Joins ArCustomer and GenMaster for descriptions.
example_questions: Drill from AR invoice INV001 to GL journal
Show GL entries behind one customer invoice
AR invoice to general ledger trace

description: Drill from a budget line to actual journal entries posted to the same account
sql: SELECT m.Company, m.GlCode, m.Description, m.AccountType, CASE %(period)s WHEN 1 THEN b.Budget1 WHEN 2 THEN b.Budget2 WHEN 3 THEN b.Budget3 WHEN 4 THEN b.Budget4 WHEN 5 THEN b.Budget5 WHEN 6 THEN b.Budget6 WHEN 7 THEN b.Budget7 WHEN 8 THEN b.Budget8 WHEN 9 THEN b.Budget9 WHEN 10 THEN b.Budget10 WHEN 11 THEN b.Budget11 WHEN 12 THEN b.Budget12 END AS BudgetForPeriod, t.JnlDate, t.Journal, t.Source, t.Reference, t.Comment, t.EntryValue, t.SubModSupplier, t.SubModCustomer, t.SubModRef FROM GenMaster m INNER JOIN GenBudgets b ON b.Company = m.Company AND b.GlCode = m.GlCode AND b.BudgetType = %(budget_type)s AND b.BudgetNumber = %(budget_number)s LEFT JOIN GenTransaction t ON t.Company = m.Company AND t.GlCode = m.GlCode AND t.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND t.GlPeriod = %(period)s WHERE m.GlCode = %(gl_code)s ORDER BY t.JnlDate, t.Journal
embedding_text: Drill into a budgeted GL account: shows the budget value for the period from GenBudgets alongside every actual GenTransaction line, including sub-module references for further drill into source documents.
example_questions: From budget account to actual journal entries
Drill budget line to underlying transactions
Variance analysis with source entries

description: Drill from a purchase order via the AP invoice to the GL postings
sql: SELECT p.Supplier, s.SupplierName, p.Invoice, p.PurchaseOrder, i.InvoiceDate, i.OrigInvValue, i.Currency, i.Branch, t.Company, t.GlCode, m.Description AS GlAccount, t.JnlDate, t.Journal, t.Source AS GlSource, t.EntryValue AS GlEntryValue, t.Reference FROM ApInvoiceRegPo p INNER JOIN ApInvoice i ON i.Supplier = p.Supplier AND i.Invoice = p.Invoice LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = p.Supplier LEFT JOIN GenTransaction t ON t.SubModApInvoice = i.Invoice AND t.SubModSupplier = i.Supplier LEFT JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = t.Company AND m.GlCode = t.GlCode WHERE p.PurchaseOrder = %(purchase_order)s ORDER BY t.JnlDate, t.Journal
embedding_text: Drill from a PO (ApInvoiceRegPo) through the AP invoice (ApInvoice) to the GL postings (GenTransaction by SubModApInvoice and SubModSupplier). Used in procure-to-pay audit and reconciliation.
example_questions: From purchase order PO456 to AP invoice to GL
P2P drill purchase order to ledger
PO trace to general ledger

description: Drill from a sales order through its AR invoice to the GL postings
keywords: sales order to ar to gl
sql: SELECT s.SalesOrder, s.Customer, c.Name AS CustomerName, s.Invoice, s.DocumentType, s.TrnDate, s.StockCode, s.StockDescription, s.InvoiceQty, s.InvoiceValue, s.CostValue, s.TaxValue, s.InvoiceCurrency, t.Company, t.GlCode, m.Description AS GlAccount, t.JnlDate, t.Journal, t.Source AS GlSource, t.EntryValue AS GlEntryValue FROM ArSalesMove s LEFT JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = s.Customer LEFT JOIN GenTransaction t ON t.SubModSalesOrder = s.SalesOrder AND t.SubModArInvoice = s.Invoice AND t.SubModCustomer = s.Customer LEFT JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = t.Company AND m.GlCode = t.GlCode WHERE s.SalesOrder = %(sales_order)s ORDER BY s.SummaryLine, s.DetailLine, t.JnlDate, t.Journal
embedding_text: Trace a sales order through ArSalesMove (which holds the SO -> invoice link) and on to GenTransaction GL entries (matched via SubModSalesOrder + SubModArInvoice + SubModCustomer). Useful for order-to-cash drill-down.
example_questions: From sales order SO123 to AR invoice to GL
Drill sales order to ledger
Order to cash trace

description: Drill from a tax return line back to the originating invoice and GL posting
sql: SELECT t.JnlYear, t.JnlMonth, t.Source, t.Journal, t.Entry, t.TaxCode, x.Description AS TaxDescription, t.InputOutPut, t.Vendor AS PartyCode, t.Invoice, t.InvoiceDate, t.InvoiceValue, t.TaxValue, t.ExemptValue, t.VatYear, t.VatPeriod, t.TaxRetSubmitted, t.SubmittedDate, i_ap.Supplier, sp.SupplierName, i_ap.Branch, i_ap.OrigInvValue, i_ap.Currency, gt.Company, gt.GlCode, gm.Description AS GlAccount, gt.EntryValue AS GlEntryValue, gt.JnlDate AS GlJnlDate FROM AdmTaxReturn t LEFT JOIN AdmTax x ON x.TaxCode = t.TaxCode LEFT JOIN ApInvoice i_ap ON i_ap.Supplier = t.Vendor AND i_ap.Invoice = t.Invoice LEFT JOIN ApSupplier sp ON sp.Supplier = i_ap.Supplier LEFT JOIN GenTransaction gt ON gt.SubModApInvoice = t.Invoice AND gt.SubModSupplier = t.Vendor LEFT JOIN GenMaster gm ON gm.Company = gt.Company AND gm.GlCode = gt.GlCode WHERE t.TaxCode = %(tax_code)s AND t.VatYear = %(vat_year)s AND t.VatPeriod = %(vat_period)s ORDER BY t.InvoiceDate DESC
embedding_text: From a tax return line in AdmTaxReturn drills back to the originating AP invoice (ApInvoice) and the GL posting (GenTransaction) for full audit of one tax-return line. Joins ApSupplier, AdmTax, and GenMaster for descriptions.
example_questions: From tax transaction to tax return line
Drill tax return line to invoice and GL
Tax return audit trail

description: Stock-on-hand value by warehouse
sql: SELECT w.Warehouse, COUNT(*) AS Items, SUM(w.QtyOnHand) AS TotalQty, SUM(w.QtyOnHand * w.UnitCost) AS StockValue FROM InvWarehouse w WHERE w.QtyOnHand <> 0 GROUP BY w.Warehouse ORDER BY StockValue DESC
embedding_text: Values inventory on hand (quantity x unit cost) per warehouse for the stock-valuation report.
example_questions: Stock on hand value by warehouse • Inventory valuation per warehouse • Closing stock value

description: Items showing negative quantity on hand
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s w.StockCode, m.Description, w.Warehouse, w.QtyOnHand, w.UnitCost, w.QtyOnHand * w.UnitCost AS StockValue FROM InvWarehouse w INNER JOIN InvMaster m ON m.StockCode = w.StockCode WHERE w.QtyOnHand < 0 ORDER BY w.QtyOnHand
embedding_text: Lists stock items with negative on-hand quantity, an inventory-control and valuation exception.
example_questions: Negative stock items • Stock with negative quantity • Inventory exceptions to investigate

description: Slow-moving stock with no issues in N months
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s m.StockCode, m.Description, m.ProductClass, w.Warehouse, w.QtyOnHand, w.QtyOnHand * w.UnitCost AS StockValue, m.DateLastSale FROM InvWarehouse w INNER JOIN InvMaster m ON m.StockCode = w.StockCode WHERE w.QtyOnHand > 0 AND (m.DateLastSale IS NULL OR m.DateLastSale < DATEADD(month, -%(months)s, GETDATE())) ORDER BY StockValue DESC
embedding_text: Identifies stock with no recent sales but positive on-hand value, supporting obsolescence-provision review.
example_questions: Slow moving stock • Obsolete inventory provision • Dead stock with no sales
